name: Spaghetti Carbonara
description: Silky egg-and-guanciale sauce on perfectly cooked spaghetti. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate more yolks for a creamier mouthfeel and use freshly toasted, hand-crushed peppercorns for superior aromatic depth.

instructions.step: Bring salted water to boil. Cook spaghetti al dente.
Toast cracked black pepper in the dry pan 30 sec until fragrant and smoking. Add guanciale and fry until crisp. Remove pan from heat.
Whisk eggs (or 2 eggs + 4 yolks for a richer, more golden sauce) with Pecorino and the toasted pepper.
Drain pasta, reserve 1 cup water. Toss hot pasta with guanciale off heat.

Add egg mix (use 2 extra yolks for a richer, more golden sauce), toss vigorously adding pasta water little by little until a silky, glossy sauce coats the pasta. No cream needed — the emulsion comes from eggs, cheese and starchy water.

name: Margherita Pizza
description: The original Neapolitan pizza — tomato, mozzarella, basil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with high-quality extra virgin olive oil and Maldon salt to amplify the simple flavor profile.

instructions.step: Make the dough: mix 400g 00-grade flour (or plain flour), 7g yeast, 1 tsp salt, 240ml warm water, 1 tbsp olive oil. Knead 8 min until smooth. Rest 1 hr until doubled. Or use 600g store-bought pizza dough.
Preheat oven to 260°C with pizza stone.
Crush tomatoes by hand, season with salt.
Stretch dough thin. Spread sauce, add torn mozzarella.
Bake 8–10 min until crust chars and cheese bubbles.
Top with fresh basil. Finish with a drizzle of high-quality extra virgin olive oil added after baking to preserve its aroma.

name: Beef Lasagna
description: Layers of rich bolognese, béchamel and pasta baked golden. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the meat with dry red wine and incorporate a finely minced mirepoix for complexity.

instructions.step: Brown beef with onion and garlic. Add tomatoes, simmer 45 min, stirring occasionally, until sauce is deep and rich.
Make béchamel: melt butter, whisk in flour, add milk, stir until thick. Season with salt, white pepper and a generous grating of fresh nutmeg.
Layer: pasta, bolognese, béchamel, cheese. Repeat twice.
Top with Parmesan. Bake at 190°C for 35–40 min.
Rest 10 min before slicing.

name: Risotto ai Funghi
description: Creamy Arborio rice with porcini mushrooms and aged Parmesan. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with fresh thyme and cold butter to enhance the aromatic profile and gloss to Michelin standards.

instructions.step: Soak porcini in hot water 20 min. Reserve soaking liquid.
Sauté shallots and garlic in butter until soft. Toast rice 3 min stirring constantly until grains are hot to touch and edges turn translucent.
Add white wine, stir vigorously until fully absorbed. Add warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring after each, 18 min.
Add mushrooms and strained soaking liquid.
Remove from heat. Add cold cubed butter and Parmesan, then agitate vigorously off-heat (mantecatura) until risotto flows like a gentle wave when the pan is shaken. Rest 1 min.

name: Osso Buco
description: Braised veal shanks in white wine — a Milanese masterpiece. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add an anchovy to the soffritto for deep umami and finish the sauce with a cold butter mount for a professional sheen.

instructions.step: Dust shanks in flour. Brown on all sides in olive oil.
Sauté onion, carrot, celery. Add garlic.
Return veal, add wine, reduce. Add tomatoes and stock to cover.
Braise at 150°C for 2.5–3 hours until the collagen has fully broken down and meat slides from the bone.
Make gremolata with lemon zest, garlic and parsley. Scatter over before serving.

name: Tiramisu
description: Espresso-soaked ladyfingers and mascarpone cream — Italy's great dessert. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a pinch of Maldon salt to the espresso to sharpen the contrast against the rich cream.

instructions.step: Beat egg yolks with sugar until pale. Fold in mascarpone.
Whip whites to stiff peaks. Fold into mascarpone.
Dip ladyfingers briefly in espresso with Marsala.
Layer soaked ladyfingers and mascarpone mixture. Dust with cocoa. Repeat layers. Refrigerate 4+ hours.
Dust with cocoa before serving.

name: Penne all'Arrabbiata
description: Fiery tomato sauce with garlic and chilli — bold and satisfying. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap flakes for fresh Calabrian chilies and finish with a high-quality, cold-pressed finishing oil.

instructions.step: Sauté garlic and chilli in olive oil until golden.
Add tomatoes, crush and simmer 15 min.
Cook penne al dente. Toss with sauce and parsley.

name: Bruschetta al Pomodoro
description: Grilled bread rubbed with garlic and topped with ripe tomatoes. ⭐ Pro Tip: Elevate with a drizzle of 25-year aged balsamic and a micro-basil garnish for visual sophistication.

instructions.step: Grill bread until charred at edges.
Rub immediately with cut garlic.
Dice tomatoes, toss with oil, basil and salt. Top bread.

name: Gnocchi al Pesto
description: Pillowy potato gnocchi tossed in vibrant Genovese pesto. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate Pecorino Sardo and finish with a squeeze of lemon to brighten the fats.

instructions.step: Blend basil, pine nuts, Parmesan, garlic and oil to smooth pesto.
Cook gnocchi until they float, about 2 min.
Reserve ½ cup cooking water. Toss with pesto, adding water to loosen.

name: Saltimbocca alla Romana
description: Veal with prosciutto and sage, pan-fried in white wine and butter. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish the sauce with cold butter off-heat for a Michelin-standard emulsion.

instructions.step: Place prosciutto and sage on each cutlet, secure with a toothpick.
Fry prosciutto-side down 2 min, flip and cook 1 min.
Remove veal. Deglaze with wine, reduce by half.
Return veal briefly and spoon sauce over.

name: Eggplant Parmigiana
description: Fried eggplant, tomato sauce and molten mozzarella baked golden. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a drizzle of premium extra-virgin olive oil and fresh basil after baking to preserve aromatic volatiles.

instructions.step: Salt eggplant slices, drain 20 min, pat dry.
Dip in egg, coat in breadcrumbs, fry until golden.
Layer in dish: sauce, eggplant, mozzarella, Parmesan. Repeat.
Bake at 190°C for 30 min.

name: Clam Linguine
description: Briny clams steamed open in white wine with garlic and parsley. ⭐ Pro Tip: Emulsify the sauce with a splash of pasta water and a knob of cold butter for a restaurant-grade finish.

instructions.step: Cook linguine al dente.
Sauté garlic and chilli. Add clams and wine.
Cover and steam until clams open, 4 min.
Toss drained pasta with clam sauce and parsley.

name: Ribollita
description: Tuscan bread and bean soup — peasant food at its finest. ⭐ Pro Tip: Let the dish rest overnight to achieve the authentic 're-boiled' complexity.

instructions.step: Sauté onion, carrot and celery in olive oil 8 min.
Add garlic, tomatoes, beans, rosemary and 1.5L water. Simmer 30 min.
Stir in kale, cook 10 min.
Add torn bread, stir to thicken. Rest 10 min.
Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil and serve.

name: Calamari Fritti
description: Light-as-air fried squid rings with lemon and aioli. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate 30% cornstarch into the flour and season with lemon zest and white pepper for elevated flavor.

instructions.step: Slice squid into rings. Make batter with flour and cold sparkling water.
Heat oil to 190°C. Dip squid in batter, fry in batches 2 min.
Drain, season with salt. Serve with lemon and aioli.

name: Panna Cotta
description: Silky vanilla cream set with gelatin, topped with berry coulis. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the vanilla bean at 65°C for 20 minutes to maximize aromatic extraction without degrading the cream's proteins.

instructions.step: Bloom gelatin in cold water 5 min.
Heat cream with sugar and vanilla until just simmering.
Add squeezed gelatin, stir to dissolve. Pour into moulds.
Refrigerate 4+ hours.
Cook berries with sugar for coulis. Serve over unmoulded panna cotta.

name: Arancini
description: Crispy fried risotto balls stuffed with mozzarella. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the rice with saffron for authentic depth and serve with a high-acid tomato coulis to cut the richness.

instructions.step: Make the risotto base: sauté 1 shallot in butter, toast 300g arborio rice, add 900ml warm vegetable broth ladle by ladle stirring 18 min. Stir in Parmesan, season well. Spread on a tray and refrigerate until completely cold (minimum 2 hrs). Or use leftover risotto.
Form cold risotto into balls around a mozzarella cube.
Roll in flour, dip in egg, coat in breadcrumbs.
Deep fry at 180°C for 4–5 min until golden.
Drain and serve hot with marinara sauce.

name: Bistecca alla Fiorentina
description: A magnificent T-bone grilled over charcoal, finished with olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce freshly cracked Tellicherry pepper and ensure the use of high-quality oak charcoal for authentic smokiness.

instructions.step: Bring steak to room temperature. Season generously with salt.
Grill over very high heat 5 min per side — rare to medium-rare.
Rest upright on the bone 10 min.
Drizzle with olive oil. Slice and serve.

name: Cannoli Siciliani
description: Crispy shells filled with sweetened ricotta and chocolate chips. ⭐ Pro Tip: Pass the filling through a fine-mesh sieve for a velvet finish and dip shell ends in tempered chocolate.

instructions.step: Make the shells: mix 200g flour, 30g sugar, 1 tsp cinnamon, 30g cold butter, 1 egg yolk and 60ml Marsala into a dough. Rest 30 min. Roll thin, cut into circles, wrap around metal tubes and deep-fry at 180°C for 3 min until golden. Cool before filling. Or use store-bought cannoli shells.
Drain ricotta in a sieve 2 hours.
Beat with powdered sugar and cinnamon until smooth.
Fold in chocolate chips and candied orange.
Pipe into shells just before serving. Dust with powdered sugar.

name: Cacio e Pepe
description: Roman perfection — pasta, Pecorino, black pepper, pasta water. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use whole peppercorns crushed immediately before toasting to preserve heat and aroma.

instructions.step: Toast cracked pepper in a dry pan until fragrant.
Cook pasta in minimal salted water. Reserve 2 cups pasta water.
Add ½ cup water to pepper in pan.
Add drained pasta. Toss off heat.
Add Pecorino and pasta water gradually until creamy.

name: Focaccia Genovese
description: Dimpled, olive oil-drenched flatbread — pillowy inside, crisp outside. ⭐ Pro Tip: Switch to a long-fermentation process for complex enzymatic development.

instructions.step: Mix flour, yeast, water, half the oil and salt. Knead 8 min.
Proof in an oiled pan 1 hour.
Dimple aggressively. Pour remaining oil over, scatter rosemary and flaky salt.
Bake at 230°C for 20–25 min until deep golden.

name: Minestrone
description: A thick seasonal vegetable soup with pasta and cannellini beans. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a bright pesto or premium extra virgin olive oil to elevate flavor and satiety.

instructions.step: Sauté onion, carrot and celery in olive oil 8 min.
Add courgette, tomatoes, Parmesan rind and 1.5L water. Simmer 20 min.
Add pasta and beans. Cook until pasta is tender, 8 min.
Add kale. Serve with grated Parmesan and olive oil.

name: Panzanella
description: Tuscan bread salad with ripe tomatoes, basil, cucumber and capers. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap water-soaking for an oven-toast with garlic to improve structural integrity.

instructions.step: Tear bread, soak briefly in water then squeeze out.
Chop tomatoes and cucumber, slice red onion thinly.
Combine bread, vegetables, capers and basil.
Dress with oil and vinegar. Rest 30 min before serving.

name: Vitello Tonnato
description: Cold roasted veal sliced thin, draped in creamy tuna mayonnaise. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace the mayonnaise with a scratch-made egg yolk and extra virgin olive oil emulsion for a luxurious texture.

instructions.step: Roast veal at 180°C until 60°C internal. Cool completely.
Blend tuna, capers, anchovies, mayo and lemon until smooth.
Slice veal very thin. Arrange on a platter.
Spoon tuna sauce over. Garnish with capers. Chill 1 hour.

name: Polenta e Funghi
description: Creamy polenta topped with sautéed wild mushrooms and truffle oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze mushrooms with dry Marsala to add acidic complexity.

instructions.step: Bring 1L salted water to boil. Whisk in polenta, cook stirring 25 min.
Stir in butter and Parmesan. Season well.
Sauté mushrooms with garlic and thyme in butter until golden.
Serve polenta topped with mushrooms and truffle oil.

name: Spaghetti alle Vongole
description: The true Neapolitan classic — clams, garlic, white wine, no cream. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a final drizzle of cold, high-phenolic olive oil and lemon zest just before serving.

instructions.step: Soak clams in cold salted water 20 min to purge sand.
Cook spaghetti al dente. Sauté garlic and chilli in oil.
Add clams and wine, cover and steam 4 min until open.
Toss pasta with clams and briny juices. Add parsley. Serve.

name: Acqua Pazza
description: Whole fish poached in crazy water — tomatoes, garlic and white wine. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a vigorous stir of cold extra virgin olive oil to transform the thin broth into a luxurious, Michelin-standard emulsion.

instructions.step: Sauté garlic in olive oil. Add tomatoes, wine, capers and 200ml water.
Add fish. Cover and cook 12–15 min.
Scatter parsley. Serve fish in its poaching broth with crusty bread.

name: Ravioli al Burro e Salvia
description: Fresh ricotta and spinach ravioli in nutty sage brown butter. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a splash of pasta water and a drop of lemon juice to balance the butter's fat.

instructions.step: Wilt spinach, squeeze dry, chop. Mix with ricotta, Parmesan and seasoning.
Roll pasta thin. Place filling in mounds, fold and cut ravioli.
Cook in boiling salted water 3 min.
Brown butter with sage leaves until nutty.
Drain ravioli, toss with sage butter and extra Parmesan.

name: Torta della Nonna
description: Grandmother's tart — shortcrust pastry, custard cream and pine nuts. Store-bought shortcrust pastry works perfectly here — or make from scratch: rub 200g cold butter into 400g flour, add 2 tbsp cold water, chill 30 min before rolling. ⭐ Pro Tip: Switch flour to cornstarch and blind-bake the pastry to ensure a crisp, golden base.

instructions.step: Make custard: whisk yolks with sugar, flour and lemon. Add hot milk, cook stirring until thick.
Line tart tin with pastry. Fill with cooled custard.
Cover with second pastry sheet, scatter pine nuts on top.
Bake at 180°C for 35 min until golden.
Dust with powdered sugar when cool.

name: Pasta e Fagioli
description: Thick pasta and bean soup — the ultimate Italian comfort bowl. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the soffritto with dry white wine and swap water for a high-quality stock to deepen the profile.

instructions.step: Sauté onion, carrot and celery in olive oil until soft.
Add garlic, tomatoes, beans, rosemary, Parmesan rind and 800ml water.
Simmer 20 min. Remove rind.
Add pasta. Cook until tender, 8 min.
Adjust consistency. Serve with a drizzle of olive oil.

name: Tagliata di Manzo
description: Sliced rested ribeye over rocket with Parmesan shavings and balsamic. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace the glaze with a 12-year aged balsamic and finish with Maldon sea salt for a sophisticated textural contrast.

instructions.step: Season steak generously. Sear in a very hot pan 3-4 min per side.
Rest 8 min.
Slice thin on the bias.
Arrange over rocket. Top with Parmesan shavings and balsamic glaze.

name: Moussaka
description: Layered eggplant, spiced lamb and creamy béchamel — the Greek classic. ⭐ Pro Tip: Roast the eggplant with a light brush of olive oil and degrease the lamb sauce before layering for a cleaner finish.

instructions.step: Slice, salt and fry eggplant until golden.
Cook lamb with onion, tomatoes and cinnamon until dry.
Make béchamel, add egg yolks.
Layer: eggplant, meat, eggplant, béchamel. Top with Parmesan.
Bake at 180°C for 45 min.
Cool 20 min before cutting.

name: Spanakopita
description: Flaky filo pastry packed with spinach and salty feta.. Store-bought filo pastry is standard. Keep sheets covered with a damp cloth as you work. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate toasted pine nuts for textural contrast and lemon zest to cut through the heavy fats.

instructions.step: Wilt spinach, squeeze dry. Mix with crumbled feta, eggs, onion and dill.
Layer 6 sheets of buttered filo in a baking tin.
Spread spinach filling. Top with 6 more buttered filo sheets. Score top.
Bake at 190°C for 35–40 min until golden.

name: Souvlaki Platter
description: Grilled pork skewers with oregano, pita, tzatziki and chips. ⭐ Pro Tip: Transition to a 24-hour dry brine before marinating to enhance moisture retention and finish with a cold-pressed finishing oil for a luxu...

instructions.step: Cut pork into cubes, marinate with oil, lemon, oregano and garlic 1 hour.
Thread onto skewers. Grill over high heat, turning, 10–12 min.
Serve with warm pita, tzatziki, sliced tomatoes and onion.

name: Greek Salad
description: Tomatoes, cucumber, olives, red onion and a slab of feta with olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a splash of aged red wine vinegar and finish with fresh flowering oregano.

instructions.step: Chop tomatoes and cucumber into chunks.
Slice onion thinly. Combine with olives.
Dress with olive oil and oregano. Place whole feta on top.

name: Tzatziki
description: Cool, garlicky cucumber yogurt — the essential Greek dip. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a pool of ultra-premium oil and a pinch of white pepper for a professional, more refined palate.

instructions.step: Grate cucumber, salt and squeeze out all liquid.
Mix yogurt with cucumber, minced garlic, dill and olive oil.
Add lemon juice and vinegar. Season. Chill 30 min.

name: Lamb Kleftiko
description: Sealed foil parcel of slow-baked lamb shoulder with lemon and herbs. ⭐ Pro Tip: Reduce the cooking juices into a concentrated jus before serving.

instructions.step: Score lamb, push garlic into slits. Marinate with lemon, oregano and oil.
Place on foil with potatoes and tomatoes. Seal tightly.
Bake at 160°C for 3 hours.
Open foil, increase to 220°C for 20 min to brown.

name: Grilled Octopus
description: Charred octopus tentacles dressed with olive oil, lemon and capers. ⭐ Pro Tip: Elevate the plating with a citrus-infused finishing oil and a pinch of Maldon salt for a true Michelin-level experience.

instructions.step: Simmer octopus in water 40 min until tender.
Cool, cut into tentacles.
Grill on very high heat 4–5 min until charred.
Dress with oil, lemon, capers and oregano.

name: Baklava
description: Layers of filo, walnuts and pistachios drenched in honey syrup.. Store-bought filo pastry is standard. Keep sheets covered with a damp cloth as you work. ⭐ Pro Tip: Clarify your butter to avoid burnt milk solids and ensure a cleaner, toasted flavor profile.

instructions.step: Chop nuts with cinnamon.
Layer buttered filo with nut mixture in a baking pan.
Score into diamonds. Bake at 170°C for 40 min.
Make syrup: simmer honey, sugar, water and cloves 5 min.
Pour hot syrup over hot baklava. Cool completely.

name: Pastitsio
description: Greek mac and cheese — pasta, spiced beef, tomatoes and béchamel. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use a beurre noisette for the béchamel to add nutty notes and temper the pasta with egg whites before layering.

instructions.step: Cook pasta al dente. Make meat sauce with beef, tomatoes and cinnamon.
Make béchamel. Whisk in egg yolks.
Layer pasta and meat in baking dish, top with béchamel and Parmesan.
Bake at 180°C for 40 min.

name: Fasolada
description: The national soup of Greece — white beans with tomatoes and herbs. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a squeeze of lemon and a dusting of smoked paprika to brighten the earthy notes.

instructions.step: Sauté onion, carrot and celery in olive oil until soft.
Add garlic, tomatoes, beans and 1L water.
Simmer 40 min until beans are very soft.
Finish with extra olive oil and parsley.

name: Saganaki
description: Pan-fried golden Kefalograviera — crispy, salty, irresistible. ⭐ Pro Tip: Balance the intense salinity with a drizzle of thyme honey and a sprinkle of toasted sesame.

instructions.step: Slice cheese 1.5cm thick. Dust lightly with flour.
Fry in hot olive oil 2 min per side until deep golden.
Transfer to plate. Squeeze lemon over. Serve immediately.

name: Dolmades
description: Grape leaves stuffed with lemony rice, herbs and pine nuts. ⭐ Pro Tip: Bloom aromatics in the oil before mixing and incorporate fresh mint for a vibrant finish.

instructions.step: Blanch grape leaves 1 min.
Mix rice with pine nuts, onion, dill, half the lemon juice and oil.
Roll filling in each leaf. Line pot with extra leaves.
Layer dolmades, add lemon juice, oil and water to cover.
Simmer 40 min covered.

name: Chicken Gyro Bowl
description: Marinated chicken with pita, tzatziki, tomatoes and pickled red onion. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap standard pita for a hand-stretched sourdough flatbread and finish with barrel-aged feta for a 5-star texture.

instructions.step: Marinate chicken with paprika, cumin, oregano, lemon and oil 30 min.
Grill 6–7 min per side until cooked through.
Rest and slice. Serve in bowls with warm pita, tzatziki and salad.

name: Stifado
description: Greek beef and shallot stew with red wine, cinnamon and cloves. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a rich beef demi-glace and finish with a cold butter emulsion for a signature glossy sheen.

instructions.step: Brown beef in batches.
Peel shallots, brown whole in same pot.
Return beef, add wine, tomatoes, vinegar and spices.
Simmer covered 80 min until beef is very tender.

name: Loukoumades
description: Greek honey doughnuts — light, crisp and drizzled with honey and cinnamon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Switch to toasted Cretan thyme honey and use premium walnuts for better texture.

instructions.step: Mix flour, yeast, water and salt to a batter. Rest 1 hour.
Drop spoonfuls into 180°C oil. Fry until golden, 3 min.
Drain and serve drizzled with honey, cinnamon and crushed walnuts.

name: Giouvetsi
description: Lamb and orzo baked together in a rich tomato sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze with dry red wine and finish with fresh lemon zest to cut the fat.

instructions.step: Brown lamb in a casserole. Add onion, garlic, tomatoes and 500ml water.
Bake covered at 180°C for 1 hour.
Stir in orzo, add more water if needed.
Bake uncovered 20 min until orzo is tender.
Top with grated Kefalograviera.

name: Taramasalata
description: Creamy Greek fish roe dip — smooth, pink and perfect with pita. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute half the olive oil with neutral grapeseed oil for a lighter, stable emulsion that won't set hard.

instructions.step: Soak bread in water, squeeze dry.
Blend tarama with bread, lemon juice and onion until smooth.
Drizzle in olive oil slowly while blending until emulsified.
Taste and adjust lemon. Chill before serving.

name: Keftedes
description: Greek herb meatballs with mint and oregano, fried until golden. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use milk-soaked sourdough instead of dry breadcrumbs for a lighter, Michelin-standard texture.

instructions.step: Mix all ingredients. Refrigerate 30 min.
Shape into balls the size of a walnut.
Dust in flour. Fry in oil 4–5 min per side until cooked through.
Serve with tzatziki and lemon wedges.

name: Spanakoryzo
description: Spinach and rice cooked in olive oil and lemon — a simple Greek staple. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with raw, high-phenolic olive oil and toasted pine nuts to add necessary textural contrast and healthy fats.

instructions.step: Sauté onion in olive oil until soft.
Add spinach in batches, wilt down.
Add rice, stir to coat. Add 400ml water.
Simmer covered 20 min until rice is cooked.
Add lemon juice and dill. Season.

name: Tiropita
description: Crispy filo triangles stuffed with feta and ricotta cheese filling.. Store-bought filo pastry is standard. Keep sheets covered with a damp cloth as you work. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate clarified butter for a cleaner flavor and higher smoke point.

instructions.step: Crumble feta, mix with ricotta, eggs and dill.
Cut filo into strips. Brush with butter.
Place a spoonful of filling at the end of each strip. Fold into triangles.
Brush with butter. Bake at 190°C for 25 min until golden.

name: Grilled Sea Bass
description: Whole sea bass grilled with lemon, olive oil and fresh herbs. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with flaky sea salt and micro-herbs to achieve a modern 5-star presentation.

instructions.step: Score fish. Season inside and out with salt, oregano and garlic.
Stuff cavity with lemon slices.
Grill 7–8 min per side until charred and cooked through.
Dress with olive oil, lemon and parsley.

name: Galaktoboureko
description: Filo pastry filled with semolina custard and soaked in lemon syrup.. Store-bought filo pastry is standard. Keep sheets covered with a damp cloth as you work. ⭐ Pro Tip: Temper eggs into the milk base to ensure a Michelin-standard, lump-free custard.

instructions.step: Make custard: heat milk with 80g sugar. Whisk in semolina, eggs, cook until thick.
Layer buttered filo in a tin. Pour in custard. Top with more filo.
Bake at 175°C for 45 min until golden.
Make syrup: boil water, remaining sugar, lemon juice 5 min. Pour over hot pastry.
Cool completely.

name: Revithia
description: Slow-cooked chickpeas baked with onion, olive oil and lemon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with fresh lemon zest and a sprig of fresh oregano at the end to maintain vibrant, volatile aromatherapeutic volatiles.

instructions.step: Soak chickpeas overnight. Drain.
Combine with onion, olive oil, herbs and lemon juice in a clay pot.
Add water to cover. Bake covered at 160°C for 90 min.
Season and serve warm with crusty bread.

name: Skordalia
description: Powerful Greek garlic dip made with mashed potato and olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a high-phenolic Greek extra virgin olive oil for a peppery finish.

instructions.step: Boil potatoes until very soft. Mash until smooth.
Blend garlic with almonds and a pinch of salt.
Combine potato with garlic paste.
Beat in olive oil and lemon juice gradually until fluffy.

name: Kolokithokeftedes
description: Crispy courgette fritters with feta, mint and lemon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap 30% of the flour for fine semolina to achieve a shatter-crisp exterior.

instructions.step: Grate courgette, salt and squeeze out all liquid.
Mix with crumbled feta, flour, eggs and herbs.
Fry spoonfuls in olive oil 3 min per side until golden.
Serve with tzatziki and lemon wedges.

name: Psarosoupa
description: Greek fish soup with vegetables and a lemony avgolemono finish. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a drizzle of premium Koroneiki olive oil and fresh dill to provide the aromatic complexity found in five-star Greek dining.

instructions.step: Simmer fish in 1.5L water with vegetables 20 min.
Remove fish, flake and set aside. Keep broth hot.
Whisk egg yolks with lemon juice. Slowly ladle in hot broth, whisking.
Return mixture and fish to pot. Heat gently (do not boil). Season.

name: Lamb Souvlaki
description: Tender cubes of marinated lamb char-grilled on skewers. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a generous dusting of dried wild oregano and Maldon salt post-rest to elevate the sensory profile.

instructions.step: Cut lamb into 3cm cubes. Marinate 2+ hours.
Thread onto skewers.
Grill over high heat 10–12 min, turning, until charred outside and pink inside.
Rest 5 min. Serve with pita, salad and tzatziki.

name: Dakos
description: Cretan barley rusk topped with grated tomato, feta and oregano. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add salt-cured capers and sea salt flakes to elevate the umami profile and balance the rich fats.

instructions.step: Briefly wet the rusks with a splash of water.
Grate tomatoes over each rusk.
Crumble feta on top.
Drizzle olive oil, scatter oregano and olives.

name: Avgolemono
description: Silky egg and lemon soup with chicken and orzo. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a bay leaf during the simmer and finish with a cold-pressed olive oil emulsion.

instructions.step: Cook orzo in chicken broth 10 min.
Whisk eggs with lemon juice.
Ladle hot broth slowly into egg mixture, whisking constantly.
Return to pot with shredded chicken. Heat gently - do not boil.
Serve immediately with parsley.

name: Spanakopita Triangles
description: Individual filo triangles stuffed with spinach and feta.. Store-bought filo pastry is standard. Keep sheets covered with a damp cloth as you work. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add lemon zest and toasted pine nuts to elevate the flavor profile and texture.

instructions.step: Wilt and squeeze spinach. Mix with feta, eggs and dill.
Cut filo into strips. Brush with butter.
Add filling, fold into triangles. Brush with butter.
Bake at 190C for 25 min.

name: Coq au Vin
description: Chicken braised in Burgundy wine with lardons, mushrooms and pearl onions. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a gelatin-rich demi-glace and finish with a cold butter mount for a glossy, professional texture.

instructions.step: Brown chicken in butter. Set aside.
Fry lardons, pearl onions and mushrooms until golden.
Add garlic, flour, wine and herbs. Return chicken.
Simmer covered 70 min until tender.

name: French Onion Soup
description: Deep caramelised onion soup crowned with a melted Gruyère croûton. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute the white wine with dry Sherry and reduce cheese by half to balance flavor and heart health.

instructions.step: Caramelise onions in butter on medium heat 45 min.
Add flour, cook 1 min. Add wine, reduce.
Add broth and thyme. Simmer 15 min.
Ladle into oven-proof bowls. Top with baguette slices and Gruyère.
Grill until cheese is bubbling and golden.

name: Beef Bourguignon
description: Beef chuck slow-braised in Burgundy with mushrooms and pearl onions. ⭐ Pro Tip: Dust the beef in seasoned flour before browning for a silken texture.

instructions.step: Brown beef in batches. Fry lardons and vegetables.
Add tomato paste, wine, garlic and herbs. Return beef.
Braise covered at 160°C for 2.5 hours.
Sauté mushrooms and onions in butter. Add to stew.
Rest 15 min. Serve with crusty bread.

name: Duck Confit
description: Duck legs cured in salt and herbs then slow-cooked in their own fat. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a single star anise to the cure for aromatic depth.

instructions.step: Rub duck with salt, pepper, garlic and herbs. Cure in fridge 24 hours.
Melt duck fat in a deep pan. Submerge duck legs.
Cook at 90°C for 2.5–3 hours until meat pulls from bone.
Remove and crisp skin in a hot pan 5 min before serving.

name: Steak Frites
description: Entrecôte steak with béarnaise sauce and crispy hand-cut frites. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a butter-basting step with aromatics during the final steak sear for superior depth and crust.

instructions.step: Cut potatoes into frites. Fry at 150°C 5 min, drain. Fry again at 190°C 3 min.
Make béarnaise: reduce vinegar with shallot and tarragon. Whisk with egg yolks over bain-marie. Emulsify with clarified butter.
Season and grill steak 3 min per side for medium-rare.
Rest 5 min. Serve with frites and béarnaise.

name: Crème Brûlée
description: Silky vanilla custard beneath a shatteringly thin caramelised sugar crust. ⭐ Pro Tip: Ensure a 24-hour chill for a firmer set before caramelizing the sugar.

instructions.step: Heat cream with split vanilla bean until just simmering.
Whisk yolks with sugar until pale. Slowly pour in hot cream.
Strain into ramekins. Bake in a water bath at 150°C for 35–40 min.
Chill 4+ hours.
Sprinkle demerara sugar and blowtorch until crackled.

name: Quiche Lorraine
description: Buttery shortcrust pastry filled with lardons, eggs, cream and Gruyère. Store-bought shortcrust pastry works well — or make from scratch: rub cold butter into flour, add iced water, chill 30 min before rolling. ⭐ Pro Tip: Rest the egg-and-cream mixture for 30 minutes to eliminate bubbles, ensuring a glass-like finish on the final custard.

instructions.step: Blind bake pastry shell at 190°C for 15 min.
Scatter lardons and Gruyère in shell.
Whisk eggs with cream, nutmeg and seasoning. Pour in.
Bake at 180°C for 30 min until just set with a slight wobble.

name: Ratatouille
description: Slow-baked Provençal vegetable tian with herbes de Provence. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with high-quality cold-pressed oil and Maldon sea salt to elevate the final flavor profile.

instructions.step: Slice all vegetables into rounds. Sauté onion and garlic.
Spread onion in a baking dish. Layer vegetables in overlapping circles.
Drizzle with oil and scatter herbs.
Cover with foil. Bake at 180°C for 40 min.
Uncover and bake 10 more min.

name: Crêpes Suzette
description: Paper-thin crêpes in a warm orange Grand Marnier butter, flambéed at the table. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a splash of Cognac with the Grand Marnier for a more complex aromatic finish.

instructions.step: Make crêpe batter: blend flour, eggs, milk and melted butter. Rest 30 min.
Cook thin crêpes in a buttered pan. Fold into quarters.
Make sauce: melt butter with sugar, orange juice and zest. Add crêpes.
Pour over Grand Marnier and flambée.

name: Escargot de Bourgogne
description: Burgundy snails baked in shells with garlic-parsley butter. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a splash of Pernod for aromatic complexity and serve atop coarse sea salt to stabilize the shells and retain heat.

instructions.step: Blend softened butter with garlic, shallot, parsley and wine.
Place a snail in each shell. Fill with compound butter.
Bake at 220°C for 8–10 min until butter is bubbling and golden.
Serve immediately with crusty baguette.

name: Bouillabaisse
description: Marseillaise saffron fish stew with rouille-topped croûtons. ⭐ Pro Tip: Emulsify the broth with premium olive oil during a hard boil for the signature texture.

instructions.step: Sauté fennel and garlic. Add tomatoes, saffron and orange peel.
Add 1L water or fish stock. Simmer 15 min.
Add fish and shellfish in order of cooking time. Simmer until all open.
Serve broth in bowls with fish alongside. Top croûtons with rouille.

name: Sole Meunière
description: Dover sole dredged in flour and pan-fried in brown butter with lemon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a sprinkle of fleur de sel and micro-parsley to meet five-star presentation standards.

instructions.step: Dust sole lightly in flour. Season.
Fry in clarified butter 3 min per side until golden.
Remove fish. Add remaining butter to pan until nut-brown and nutty.
Add lemon juice and parsley. Pour over fish immediately.

name: Cassoulet
description: White bean and duck confit casserole with Toulouse sausage — the dish of Languedoc. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate rendered duck fat into the bean simmering process for a true Michelin-level finish.

instructions.step: Soak beans overnight. Simmer until just tender, 45 min.
Brown all meats separately.
Layer beans and meats in a cassole. Cover with tomato broth.
Bake uncovered at 160°C for 2 hours, breaking crust and adding broth every 30 min.
Finish with breadcrumb crust, bake 15 min until golden.

name: Tarte Tatin
description: Caramelised upside-down apple tart with buttery puff pastry. Store-bought puff pastry is standard — or make from scratch by folding butter into flour dough 6 times with resting periods between each turn. ⭐ Pro Tip: Reduce apple juices post-baking to create a concentrated glaze for a professional, mirror-like finish.

instructions.step: Melt butter and sugar in an oven-proof pan until amber caramel.
Arrange halved apples tightly in caramel.
Drape puff pastry over apples, tuck edges in.
Bake at 200°C for 25 min until pastry is golden.
Cool 5 min, then invert onto a plate. Serve with crème fraîche.

name: Vichyssoise
description: Chilled cream of leek and potato soup — elegant and silky smooth. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a squeeze of lemon juice or a drop of white wine vinegar just before serving to cut through the heavy lipids and brighten the palate.

instructions.step: Sauté sliced leeks in butter until soft.
Add diced potato and broth. Simmer 20 min.
Blend until very smooth. Add cream.
Chill completely.
Serve cold topped with chives.

name: Île Flottante
description: Soft meringue islands floating on crème anglaise with caramel. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate toasted almond slivers to provide a critical textural counterpoint to the soft components.

instructions.step: Whip whites with 120g sugar to stiff peaks.
Poach spoonfuls in simmering milk 2 min each side. Set aside.
Make crème anglaise with yolks, milk and 80g sugar.
Make caramel with remaining 60g sugar and 2 tbsp water.
Pool crème anglaise in bowls, float meringue, drizzle caramel.

name: Croissants
description: Flaky, buttery laminated pastry — the Platonic ideal of breakfast. ⭐ Pro Tip: Ensure a final proof temperature of 26-28°C to avoid melting the internal butter layers before baking.

instructions.step: Make a dough with flour, milk, sugar, yeast and salt. Chill overnight.
Laminate with cold butter: fold and roll 3 times with 30 min rests between.
Roll, cut triangles, roll into crescents.
Proof 2 hours until puffy.
Brush with egg wash. Bake at 200°C for 18 min until mahogany.

name: Soufflé au Fromage
description: Cloud-light baked cheese soufflé — serve immediately at the table. ⭐ Pro Tip: Reduce temperature to 175°C for a more uniform, stable rise.

instructions.step: Make béchamel with butter, flour and milk. Add Gruyère and mustard.
Whisk egg whites to stiff peaks.
Beat yolks into sauce. Fold in whites in three additions.
Pour into buttered ramekins dusted with Parmesan.
Bake at 190°C for 12 min — do not open the oven!

name: Magret de Canard
description: Duck breast scored and pan-seared to render fat, served with cherry sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Ensure you discard excess rendered fat before deglazing to prevent a greasy emulsion.

instructions.step: Score duck skin in a crosshatch. Season generously.
Start skin-side down in a cold pan. Render fat on medium 10 min.
Flip and cook 4 min for medium-rare.
Rest 10 min.
Make sauce: deglaze with wine, add shallot, thyme and cherry jam. Finish with butter.
Slice thinly and serve with sauce.

name: Mousse au Chocolat
description: Rich, airy dark chocolate mousse — simple perfection. ⭐ Pro Tip: Fold in a teaspoon of espresso powder and finish with fleur de sel for a modern, complex edge.

instructions.step: Melt chocolate with butter. Cool slightly.
Separate eggs. Beat yolks with sugar until pale.
Fold chocolate into yolk mixture.
Whip whites with salt to stiff peaks.
Fold whites into chocolate in three additions.
Chill 4+ hours.

name: Pâté de Campagne
description: Rustic country terrine of pork liver, pork shoulder and aromatics. ⭐ Pro Tip: Lower the oven to 145°C for a silkier finish and incorporate a splash of heavy cream for better moisture retention.

instructions.step: Mince liver and pork together coarsely.
Mix with shallot, garlic, cognac, thyme, allspice and eggs.
Press into a terrine lined with lardons.
Bake in a water bath at 160°C for 80 min.
Press with weights. Chill overnight.
Serve sliced with cornichons and crusty bread.

name: Mille-Feuille
description: Crisp puff pastry layered with vanilla pastry cream and iced with fondant. Store-bought puff pastry is standard — or make from scratch by folding butter into flour dough 6 times with resting periods between each turn. ⭐ Pro Tip: Caramelize the puff pastry layers with icing sugar before assembly to create a moisture barrier and enhance crunch.

instructions.step: Bake puff pastry sheets at 200°C until golden. Press flat.
Make pastry cream with yolks, sugar, flour and milk.
Cut pastry into three equal layers.
Layer: pastry sheet, pastry cream, pastry sheet, pastry cream, final pastry sheet.
Ice top with fondant and pipe chocolate lines for feathered pattern.

name: Salade Niçoise
description: Tuna, green bean, potato, egg, olive and anchovy salad with mustard vinaigrette. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with fleur de sel and fresh-cracked white pepper to highlight the sweetness of the blanched beans.

instructions.step: Boil potatoes and eggs separately. Blanch green beans.
Make vinaigrette: whisk mustard, vinegar and olive oil.
Arrange all ingredients on a platter.
Drizzle with vinaigrette. Top with anchovies.

name: Gratin Dauphinois
description: Thinly sliced potatoes baked in cream and Gruyère until golden. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the cream with smashed garlic and thyme over low heat before assembly for a more sophisticated profile.

instructions.step: Rub baking dish with garlic, then butter.
Layer potato slices, seasoning and cream.
Top with Gruyère and a grating of nutmeg.
Bake at 170°C for 55–60 min until potatoes are tender and top is golden.

name: Blanquette de Veau
description: Veal in a creamy white sauce with mushrooms and pearl onions. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the simmering liquid with white peppercorns and a splash of Sauvignon Blanc for depth.

instructions.step: Blanch veal 5 min. Drain. Simmer in fresh water with carrot and bouquet garni 60 min.
Sauté mushrooms and pearl onions in butter.
Make velouté with butter, flour and 500ml cooking liquid.
Add cream and egg yolks. Do not boil.
Combine veal, vegetables and sauce. Add lemon juice.

name: Moules Marinières
description: Mussels steamed open in white wine, shallots and parsley. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a squeeze of lemon and monter au beurre with cold cubes to create a professional glossy emulsion.

instructions.step: Scrub and debeard mussels. Discard any that stay open.
Sauté shallot and garlic in butter until soft.
Add wine and mussels. Cover and cook 4–5 min until all open.
Stir in cream. Scatter parsley.
Serve immediately with crusty bread and the steaming broth.

name: Tarte Flambée (Flammkuchen)
description: Alsatian thin-crust tart with crème fraîche, onion and lardons. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a micro-grating of fresh nutmeg and a crack of Tellicherry black pepper post-bake for aromatic complexity.

instructions.step: Make the dough: mix 250g plain flour, ½ tsp salt, 125ml water, 2 tbsp olive oil. Knead 3 min until smooth. Rest 20 min. Or use store-bought pizza dough rolled very thin.
Roll dough as thin as possible.
Spread crème fraîche, season with nutmeg.
Top with sliced onion and lardons.
Bake at 250°C for 10–12 min until edges char.
Serve immediately, cut into rectangles.

name: Clafoutis
description: Cherries baked in a thick, custardy batter — a Limousin summer classic. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap 100ml of milk for heavy cream to achieve a silkier, Michelin-standard custard mouthfeel.

instructions.step: Pit cherries (or leave pits in, traditionally). Butter a baking dish and add cherries.
Blend eggs, milk, sugar, flour and vanilla until smooth.
Pour batter over cherries.
Bake at 180°C for 35–40 min until puffed and golden.
Dust with powdered sugar. Serve warm.

name: Gratin de Chou-fleur
description: Cauliflower baked in a Gruyère béchamel until golden and bubbling. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap steaming for roasting with garlic and thyme to concentrate flavor and eliminate excess moisture.

instructions.step: Steam cauliflower florets 5 min. Arrange in a baking dish.
Make béchamel with butter, flour and milk. Add mustard, nutmeg and half the cheese.
Pour sauce over cauliflower. Top with remaining cheese.
Bake at 200°C for 25 min until golden.

name: Pot-au-Feu
description: The great French boiled dinner — beef, marrow bones and winter vegetables. ⭐ Pro Tip: Extend the beef simmer to 4 hours and introduce a bouquet garni for complexity.

instructions.step: Stud onion with cloves. Combine brisket, marrow bones, onion, celery and herbs in a large pot.
Cover with cold water. Bring slowly to simmer, skimming foam.
Simmer gently 90 min.
Add carrots, leeks and turnips. Cook another 30 min until all vegetables are tender.
Serve broth as a first course. Then serve meats and vegetables with cornichons and mustard.

name: Paella Valenciana
description: The original Valencian paella with chicken, rabbit and green beans. ⭐ Pro Tip: Toast saffron threads lightly to amplify floral notes before incorporating.

instructions.step: Brown meat in olive oil in paella pan.
Add tomato and paprika. Cook 3 min.
Add rice, stir to coat. Add saffron broth.
Arrange beans. Do not stir again. Cook on medium 18 min.
Rest uncovered 5 min to form socarrat crust.

name: Gazpacho
description: Ice-cold blended raw vegetable soup — the taste of Andalusia in summer. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a drizzle of Arbequina olive oil for a delicate, fruity aroma.

instructions.step: Soak bread in water. Squeeze dry.
Blend all vegetables, bread, vinegar and oil until very smooth.
Pass through a sieve. Season.
Chill at least 2 hours.
Serve with diced cucumber, pepper and crouton garnishes.

name: Tortilla Española
description: Thick Spanish omelette with slow-cooked potato and onion. ⭐ Pro Tip: Hydrate the potato-egg mixture for 15 minutes before frying to ensure a creamy, cohesive interior.

instructions.step: Slice potato and onion thinly. Confit slowly in olive oil at 120°C for 20 min.
Drain, reserving oil. Cool slightly.
Beat eggs with salt. Fold in potato mixture.
Cook in a non-stick pan on medium 4 min.
Flip with a plate. Slide back in. Cook 3 min.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Patatas Bravas
description: Cubed fried potatoes with a smoky bravas sauce and aioli. ⭐ Pro Tip: Cold-start the potatoes or soak them for 24 hours to maximize starch removal and final crispness.

instructions.step: Fry potato cubes at 180°C for 15 min until golden.
Make bravas sauce: fry garlic, add tomatoes, paprika, cayenne and vinegar. Simmer 10 min.
Blend sauce smooth.
Toss potatoes with sauce. Serve with aioli.

name: Gambas al Ajillo
description: Prawns sizzling in garlic, chilli and sherry-spiked olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the oil with prawn heads for five minutes before searing the meat to elevate the dish.

instructions.step: Heat olive oil with garlic and chilli until garlic is golden.
Add prawns. Cook 2 min per side.
Add sherry, let sizzle 30 sec.
Scatter parsley. Serve sizzling with crusty bread.

name: Croquetas de Jamón
description: Creamy béchamel and jamón croquettes, breadcrumbed and fried golden. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the milk with ham bones and aromatics for 20 minutes before straining for deeper flavor.

instructions.step: Make very thick béchamel with butter, flour and milk. Add diced jamón and nutmeg.
Chill dough 3+ hours until firm.
Shape into cylinders. Dip in egg then breadcrumbs.
Fry at 180°C for 3 min until golden.

name: Pulpo a la Gallega
description: Galician boiled octopus with smoked paprika, sea salt and olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a splash of sherry vinegar to cut through the richness and use flaky sea salt for textural contrast.

instructions.step: Boil octopus: dip in boiling water 3 times, then simmer 40 min.
Cook sliced potatoes in same water 10 min.
Slice octopus into rounds.
Arrange potato on a wooden board, top with octopus.
Drizzle with olive oil, dust with paprika and sea salt.

name: Pan con Tomate
description: Grilled bread rubbed with tomato and garlic, drizzled with olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Temper the Jamón to 25°C before serving to melt the intramuscular fat.

instructions.step: Grill or toast bread slices until charred on edges.
Rub immediately with cut garlic, then a cut tomato (halved), squeezing juice into bread.
Drizzle with olive oil and season with sea salt.
Top with Jamón Ibérico if desired.

name: Cochinillo Asado
description: Whole suckling pig roasted until the skin shatters like glass. ⭐ Pro Tip: Air-dry the pig uncovered in the fridge for 24 hours prior to roasting to ensure an ultra-thin, shatteringly crisp skin.

instructions.step: Rub pig all over with lard, garlic and salt.
Roast skin-side up at 160°C for 1.5 hours basting with wine.
Increase to 220°C for 20–30 min until skin is shatteringly crisp.
Carve with the edge of a plate — tradition says the knife is unnecessary.

name: Arroz Negro
description: Black squid ink rice with squid, saffron and garlic aioli. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with fresh lemon zest and micro-herbs to brighten the heavy, ink-stained rice.

instructions.step: Sauté garlic and squid 3 min.
Add rice, paprika and wine. Cook until absorbed.
Add squid ink and fish broth. Cook without stirring 18 min.
Add prawns for final 5 min.
Serve with a generous spoonful of aioli.

name: Fabada Asturiana
description: Asturian white bean and smoked pork stew — the Spanish cassoulet. ⭐ Pro Tip: Balance the heavy fats by finishing with a high-quality, acidic cider vinegar to brighten the palate.

instructions.step: Soak beans overnight. Drain.
Cover with fresh water with meats, garlic and bay leaf. Bring to boil.
Add saffron and paprika. Simmer 1.5–2 hours until beans are very soft.
Remove meats, slice and return.
Season. Serve as a stew with crusty bread.

name: Boquerones en Vinagre
description: Fresh anchovies marinated in white wine vinegar with garlic and parsley. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with a whisper of zest and Maldon salt just before serving to heighten the clean, maritime flavors.

instructions.step: Fillet anchovies. Lay flat in a dish.
Cover with vinegar. Marinate in fridge 4–6 hours until flesh turns white.
Drain and rinse.
Drizzle with olive oil, add sliced garlic and parsley.
Serve immediately with bread and olives.

name: Ibérico Ham Board
description: Hand-carved Pata Negra with Manchego, olives and quince paste. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate 'Pan con Tomate' with fresh tomato pulp and premium Arbequina oil for an authentic texture.

instructions.step: Slice Manchego into wedges.
Arrange jamón, cheese, olives, quince and almonds on a wooden board.
Serve with warm bread.

name: Churros con Chocolate
description: Crispy fried dough sticks dipped in thick Castilian hot chocolate. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a cornstarch slurry into the chocolate for a silkier, thicker finish.

instructions.step: Bring water, oil and salt to a boil. Remove from heat, beat in flour.
Pipe dough through a star nozzle into 180°C oil. Fry 3 min.
Drain. Toss in cinnamon sugar.
Make chocolate: simmer milk, melt in chocolate and sugar until thick.
Serve churros with hot chocolate for dipping.

name: Crema Catalana
description: The original crème brûlée — custard scented with lemon and cinnamon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the citrus zest overnight in cold milk before heating to extract more volatile oils without bitterness.

instructions.step: Heat milk with lemon zest, orange zest and cinnamon until simmering.
Whisk yolks with sugar and cornstarch until pale.
Pour hot milk over yolks, whisking. Return to pan, stir until thick.
Pour into ramekins. Chill 4 hours.
Sprinkle demerara sugar and caramelise with a blowtorch.

name: Salmorejo
description: Thick Córdoba-style tomato and bread soup served with jamón and egg. ⭐ Pro Tip: Rub garlic with salt to create a paste first, tempering its raw pungency for a more sophisticated flavor profile.

instructions.step: Blend tomatoes, soaked bread, garlic and vinegar until very smooth.
Drizzle in olive oil with blender running.
Pass through a sieve. Chill 1 hour.
Serve in bowls garnished with chopped egg and torn jamón.

name: Pisto Manchego
description: La Mancha ratatouille with courgette, peppers, aubergine and tomato. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the base with a splash of Sherry vinegar to provide a sharp acidic counterpoint to the rich, oil-forward profile.

instructions.step: Fry each vegetable separately in olive oil until soft. Set aside.
Fry onion and garlic. Add tomatoes and sugar.
Combine all vegetables. Simmer 20 min.
Serve warm or cold with crusty bread. Fry an egg per person in olive oil and top each portion for a more substantial meal.

name: Fideuà
description: Paella made with short toasted noodles instead of rice. ⭐ Pro Tip: Sear seafood separately and add back at the very end to prevent a rubbery texture.

instructions.step: Toast fideos in olive oil until golden.
Add garlic and paprika. Add saffron broth.
Lay seafood on top. Cook without stirring 15 min.
Finish under grill 3 min to crisp noodles.
Serve with aioli.

name: Tarta de Santiago
description: Galician almond cake decorated with the Cross of Saint James. ⭐ Pro Tip: Lightly toast the ground almonds to unlock deeper aromatic complexity before mixing.

instructions.step: Beat eggs with sugar until pale.
Fold in ground almonds, citrus zest and cinnamon.
Pour into a greased 24cm tin.
Bake at 180°C for 35 min until golden and set.
Dust with powdered sugar through a Santiago Cross stencil.

name: Callos a la Madrileña
description: Madrid tripe stew with chorizo, morcilla and chickpeas. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze with a dry Sherry (Fino or Amontillado) to elevate acidity and aromatic complexity.

instructions.step: Boil tripe until tender, 1.5 hours. Slice.
Fry onion, garlic and paprika.
Add tomatoes, chickpeas, chorizo and morcilla.
Add tripe and enough broth to cover. Simmer 1 hour.
Finish with fresh mint.

name: Leche Frita
description: Fried milk custard squares coated in cinnamon sugar — Spain's comfort dessert. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse milk with whole cinnamon and vanilla bean for 30 minutes before thickening.

instructions.step: Heat milk with lemon zest. Whisk in flour, cornstarch and 80g sugar. Cook until very thick, 8 min.
Pour into a greased tray. Chill 2 hours until firm.
Cut into squares. Dip in beaten egg then breadcrumbs.
Fry at 180°C until golden, 2 min per side.
Roll in cinnamon sugar. Serve warm.

name: Bacalao a la Vizcaína
description: Salt cod in a rich Basque sauce of choricero peppers and tomato. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a splash of txakoli wine for acidity and finish with premium Arbequina olive oil for a glossy sheen.

instructions.step: Soak salt cod 48 hours, changing water. Poach until tender.
Soak dried choricero peppers 30 min. Scrape flesh.
Sauté onion and garlic. Add tomatoes and pepper flesh. Simmer 15 min.
Add cod. Simmer 8 min.
Serve with crusty bread.

name: Pintxos Platter
description: Basque bar snacks — bread rounds topped with various pintxos. ⭐ Pro Tip: A finishing touch of high-phenolic olive oil and smoked paprika will bridge the flavors elegantly.

instructions.step: Slice baguette into rounds. Brush with olive oil, toast lightly.
Hard boil and slice eggs.
Top each bread with varied combinations: jamón, cheese, anchovies, pepper, egg.
Secure toppings with a cocktail skewer.
Serve with aioli and cold Txakoli wine.

name: Sopa de Ajo
description: Castilian garlic soup with paprika, stale bread and a poached egg. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap beef broth for a clear poultry stock infused with ham hock.

instructions.step: Slice garlic and fry in olive oil until golden.
Add paprika and diced bread. Toss to coat.
Add hot broth. Simmer 10 min.
Crack eggs into soup. Poach 3 min.
Serve with parsley.

name: Escalivada
description: Catalan chargrilled aubergine and pepper salad with olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with Maldon sea salt and Cantabrian anchovies for essential umami contrast.

instructions.step: Roast whole aubergine, peppers and onions directly over flame or at 220°C for 45 min.
Place in a bag to steam 10 min.
Peel and tear into strips.
Dress with garlic, olive oil and sherry vinegar.
Serve at room temperature with anchovies and bread.

name: Albóndigas en Salsa
description: Spanish pork and beef meatballs simmered in a tomato almond sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the pan with a dry Manzanilla sherry for superior acidity and depth.

instructions.step: Soak bread in milk. Mix with meats, egg, garlic and parsley. Shape into balls.
Brown meatballs all over.
Fry garlic and add tomatoes and wine. Simmer 5 min.
Blend sauce with almonds and saffron until smooth.
Add meatballs. Simmer 15 min.

name: Porra Antequerana
description: A thicker, richer version of gazpacho from Antequera. ⭐ Pro Tip: Blanch and skin tomatoes to eliminate bitterness and ensure a refined mouthfeel; garnish with premium ventresca tuna for luxury.

instructions.step: Soak bread. Blend with tomatoes, pepper, garlic and vinegar until thick.
Drizzle in olive oil. Season.
Chill 1 hour.
Serve topped with chopped egg and tuna.

name: Espinacas con Garbanzos
description: Sevillian spinach and chickpeas with cumin and smoked paprika. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with high-quality extra virgin olive oil and a dash more vinegar for acidity.

instructions.step: Fry garlic in olive oil until golden. Add paprika and cumin.
Add chickpeas and fry 3 min.
Add spinach, wilt down.
Add sherry vinegar. Season.

name: Pimientos de Padron
description: Blistered Galician peppers fried in olive oil - most mild, some fiery. ⭐ Pro Tip: Utilize a heavy cast-iron skillet for superior thermal mass and more aggressive charring.

instructions.step: Heat olive oil in a pan until very hot.
Add peppers. Fry on high heat 3-4 min, tossing, until blistered.
Transfer to a plate. Shower with sea salt.
Eat immediately.

name: Secreto Iberico
description: Hidden cut of Iberico pork - deeply marbled, grilled and sliced. ⭐ Pro Tip: Balance the richness with a side of charred Shishito peppers or a sharp Romesco sauce.

instructions.step: Season generously. Grill over very high heat 3 min per side.
Rest 5 min.
Slice thin against grain.
Drizzle with olive oil and lemon.

name: Al Pastor Tacos
description: Marinated pork with pineapple on corn tortillas — Mexico City's king taco. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use a cast-iron skillet for a 'plancha' finish to achieve the necessary Maillard reaction on thin slices.

instructions.step: (Store-bought corn tortillas are standard and preferred — warm on a dry griddle. To make from scratch: mix 200g masa harina, ½ tsp salt, 240ml warm water; press into thin rounds and cook 1 min per side.)  Blend achiote, rehydrated guajillo, garlic and pineapple juice. Marinate pork overnight.
Grill pork until charred and cooked through.
Slice thin with pineapple chunks.
Serve on warm tortillas with onion, cilantro and lime.

name: Birria Tacos
description: Braised beef cheek tacos dipped in rich consommé — crispy, melty, unforgettable. ⭐ Pro Tip: Fortify the consommé with roasted marrow bones for a luxurious, five-star finish.

instructions.step: (Store-bought corn tortillas are standard and preferred — warm on a dry griddle. To make from scratch: mix 200g masa harina, ½ tsp salt, 240ml warm water; press into thin rounds and cook 1 min per side.)  Toast and soak dried chillies. Blend with garlic and spices.
Coat beef in chilli paste. Braise 3 hours at 160°C.
Shred beef. Reserve consommé.
Dip tortillas in consommé, fill with beef and cheese.
Fry on griddle until crispy. Serve with consommé for dipping.

name: Chicken Enchiladas
description: Corn tortillas rolled with chicken in red sauce and melted cheese. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the sauce with toasted guajillo chiles for smoky complexity and finish with pickled red onions to cut through the heavy fats.

instructions.step: (Store-bought corn tortillas are standard and preferred — warm on a dry griddle. To make from scratch: mix 200g masa harina, ½ tsp salt, 240ml warm water; press into thin rounds and cook 1 min per side.)  Poach and shred chicken. Mix with onion and ½ cup sauce.
Warm tortillas. Fill with chicken, roll and place in baking dish.
Pour remaining sauce over. Top with cheese.
Bake at 190°C for 20 min until bubbly.
Serve with crema and cotija.

name: Guacamole
description: Chunky avocado dip with lime, jalapeño and cilantro — the essential. ⭐ Pro Tip: Ditch the excessive chips for toasted pepitas or crudités to maintain nutritional integrity.

instructions.step: Halve avocados, remove stone. Scoop flesh into a bowl.
Mash with a fork — leave some chunks.
Add lime juice, salt, diced jalapeño, onion, tomato and cilantro.
Adjust seasoning. Serve immediately with chips.

name: Carnitas Burrito
description: Slow-cooked braised pork wrapped in a flour tortilla with rice and beans. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate pickled red onions or a lime-heavy cabbage slaw to provide acid and enzymatic digestion support against the heavy fats.

instructions.step: (Store-bought flour tortillas are standard. To make from scratch: mix 240g flour, ½ tsp salt, 1 tsp baking powder, 2 tbsp lard, 140ml warm water; knead, rest 30 min, roll thin and cook on a dry griddle 1 min per side.)  (For the braised pork carnitas: season 1kg pork shoulder with cumin, oregano, garlic, orange juice and salt. Braise covered at 160°C for 3 hrs, shred, then crisp under the broiler.) Warm carnitas in a pan until slightly crispy.
Warm tortillas. Layer rice, beans, carnitas, cheese, salsa, crema and guacamole in center.
Fold sides in, then roll from bottom — tight!
Toast on a dry griddle seam-side down until sealed.

name: Chiles Rellenos
description: Poblano peppers stuffed with cheese, battered and fried in egg — with tomato sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a pinch of salt and baking powder to the flour for better seasoning and a lighter, aerated crust.

instructions.step: Char peppers over flame. Steam in bag 10 min. Peel and slit to remove seeds.
Fill with cheese. Close and dust in flour.
Beat egg whites to stiff peaks. Fold in yolks.
Dip peppers in egg batter. Fry until golden, 3 min per side.
Serve in tomato sauce with crema.

name: Pozole Rojo
description: Pork and hominy corn soup in a rich guajillo broth — a Mexican Sunday ritual. ⭐ Pro Tip: Upgrade the broth by blooming whole spices in lard before blending and triple the simmering time for the pork.

instructions.step: Simmer pork with garlic and oregano 1 hour. Shred.
Toast and soak guajillo chillies. Blend with garlic and broth.
Add chilli purée and hominy to broth. Simmer 20 min.
Serve with toppings: cabbage, radish, oregano, lime and tostadas.

name: Shrimp Ceviche
description: Shrimp "cooked" in citrus juice with tomato, jalapeño and cucumber. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with micro-greens and radish slivers for the requisite visual and textural complexity.

instructions.step: Cut shrimp into thirds. Submerge in lime and lemon juice.
Marinate 15 min until pink and opaque.
Drain half the juice. Add diced onion, tomato, jalapeño and cucumber.
Fold in cilantro and avocado. Serve on tostadas.

name: Mole Negro with Chicken
description: Dark, complex Oaxacan mole with chocolate, chillies and dozens of spices. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate Pasilla chillies and charred bread to achieve a darker, more sophisticated lacquer.

instructions.step: Toast and soak dried chillies. Toast sesame and peanuts.
Char tomatoes, onion and garlic.
Blend everything with chocolate and spices to a smooth paste.
Fry paste in oil until darkened, 10 min.
Add broth, simmer 30 min. Add chicken. Cook 20 more min.

name: Tamales
description: Masa steamed in corn husks filled with chicken mole or chile pork. ⭐ Pro Tip: Upgrade to fresh masa and incorporate a touch of toasted cumin or smoked lard for complexity.

instructions.step: Soak corn husks in hot water 30 min.
Beat lard until fluffy. Mix with masa, broth, baking powder and salt.
Spread masa on husks. Add filling. Fold and tie.
Steam upright for 1 hour until masa pulls away from husk.

name: Elote
description: Grilled corn on the cob with mayo, cotija, chile powder and lime. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate smoked paprika for depth and use a microplane for the cotija to achieve a sophisticated, snow-like texture.

instructions.step: Grill corn over high heat 8–10 min, turning, until charred.
Brush with mayo while hot.
Roll in cotija, dust with chile powder.
Squeeze lime and scatter cilantro.

name: Tres Leches Cake
description: Sponge cake soaked in three milks — light, dreamy and impossibly moist. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a stabilized chantilly and fresh berries to provide essential acidity.

instructions.step: Beat eggs and sugar until ribbon stage. Fold in flour.
Bake at 180°C for 25 min.
Poke all over with a skewer. Pour mixed three milks over warm cake. Chill 4 hours.
Whip cream and spread over. Dust with cinnamon.

name: Steak Fajitas
description: Sizzling skirt steak with peppers and onion served in warm flour tortillas. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a fermented element like pickled red onions to cut through the fat and provide visual contrast.

instructions.step: (Store-bought flour tortillas are standard. To make from scratch: mix 240g flour, ½ tsp salt, 1 tsp baking powder, 2 tbsp lard, 140ml warm water; knead, rest 30 min, roll thin and cook on a dry griddle 1 min per side.)  Marinate steak with lime, cumin, paprika and garlic 30 min.
Cook steak on screaming hot cast iron 3 min per side.
Rest 5 min, slice against grain.
Fry peppers and onion in same pan until charred.
Serve sizzling on cast iron with warm tortillas, guacamole and sour cream.

name: Churros
description: Crispy fried dough sticks rolled in cinnamon sugar with chocolate sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute vegetable oil with browned butter and incorporate eggs to achieve a lighter, more sophisticated crumb.

instructions.step: Bring water, oil and salt to boil. Beat in flour.
Pipe through a star tip into 180°C oil. Fry 3 min.
Roll in cinnamon sugar.
Make chocolate sauce: heat cream, pour over chocolate. Stir smooth.
Serve churros with chocolate dipping sauce.

name: Horchata
description: Creamy Mexican rice and cinnamon drink — refreshing and comforting. ⭐ Pro Tip: Toast the cinnamon and rice to unlock volatile oils before soaking.

instructions.step: Soak rice with cinnamon in water overnight.
Blend until smooth.
Strain through a fine-mesh sieve or muslin cloth twice until completely smooth.
Add sugar and vanilla. Stir until dissolved.
Serve over ice.

name: Agua Fresca de Jamaica
description: Deep ruby hibiscus flower agua fresca — tart, floral and refreshing. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the syrup with star anise or cinnamon to add aromatic depth.

instructions.step: Simmer hibiscus in water 10 min.
Strain. Add sugar and stir until dissolved.
Chill completely.
Serve over ice with lime juice.

name: Enfrijoladas
description: Corn tortillas dipped in a smooth black bean sauce with cheese and crema. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse beans with charred onion and avocado leaf, then finish with lime-macerated onions to cut the richness.

instructions.step: (Store-bought corn tortillas are standard and preferred — warm on a dry griddle. To make from scratch: mix 200g masa harina, ½ tsp salt, 240ml warm water; press into thin rounds and cook 1 min per side.)  Blend beans with chipotle and garlic until smooth.
Heat bean sauce in a wide pan. Dip each tortilla briefly.
Fold into quarters and arrange on plates.
Top with crumbled queso fresco, drizzle of crema, onion and cilantro.

name: Camarones a la Diabla
description: Prawns in a fiery red diabla sauce with garlic and tomatoes. ⭐ Pro Tip: Pass the sauce through a chinois for a silky, restaurant-grade mouthfeel and deglaze with a splash of dry white wine or shrimp-head bouil...

instructions.step: Toast and soak guajillo chillies. Blend with chipotle, tomatoes and garlic.
Fry sauce in butter 5 min until darkened.
Add prawns. Cook 5 min until pink.
Squeeze lime. Serve with rice.

name: Sopa de Lima
description: Yucatecan chicken and lime soup with crispy tortilla strips. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the broth with Mexican oregano and a hint of clove for aromatic depth.

instructions.step: (Store-bought corn tortillas are standard and preferred — warm on a dry griddle. To make from scratch: mix 200g masa harina, ½ tsp salt, 240ml warm water; press into thin rounds and cook 1 min per side.)  Char tomato, onion and garlic under broiler.
Simmer chicken in broth 15 min. Shred.
Add charred vegetables and lime juice. Simmer 8 min.
Fry tortilla strips in oil until crispy.
Serve soup topped with tortilla strips and cilantro.

name: Rajas con Crema
description: Roasted poblano strips in cream with onion and corn — a taco filling classic. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with charred corn husks or micro-herbs to provide visual elevation and aromatic lift.

instructions.step: Char poblanos over flame. Steam, peel and slice into strips.
Sauté onion and garlic in butter.
Add corn and poblano strips. Cook 3 min.
Add crema and shredded manchego. Simmer 5 min until cheese melts into the sauce.

name: Coctel de Camarón
description: Mexican shrimp cocktail in a tangy tomato sauce with avocado and hot sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with micro-cilantro and a hint of smoked sea salt for visual and sensory impact.

instructions.step: Mix Clamato, ketchup, hot sauce and lime juice.
Add diced cucumber, onion and cilantro.
Add shrimp. Chill 15 min.
Serve in tall glasses topped with avocado. Accompany with tostadas.

name: Flautas de Pollo
description: Crispy rolled corn tortilla flutes filled with shredded chicken. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce a lime-marinated cabbage slaw for crucial acidity and crunch; season the chicken filling with toasted spices to meet elite stan...

instructions.step: (Store-bought corn tortillas are standard and preferred — warm on a dry griddle. To make from scratch: mix 200g masa harina, ½ tsp salt, 240ml warm water; press into thin rounds and cook 1 min per side.)  Warm tortillas on a dry griddle until pliable, about 30 sec each.
Fill each with chicken, roll tightly and secure with toothpick.
Fry in hot oil at 175°C until golden and crisp, 3–4 min per batch.
Serve over shredded lettuce with sour cream, salsa verde, avocado and cotija.

name: Caldo Tlalpeño
description: Smoky chipotle and chickpea chicken broth — a Mexico City cold-weather staple. ⭐ Pro Tip: Char the onion and garlic over an open flame before simmering to add smoky complexity.

instructions.step: Simmer chicken with onion, garlic and carrot 20 min.
Shred chicken. Add chickpeas, chipotle and epazote.
Simmer 12 min.
Season. Serve with lime and tortillas.

name: Pan Dulce Conchas
description: Soft Mexican sweet bread with a crunchy colored sugar crust. ⭐ Pro Tip: Implement a cold bulk ferment to enhance dough complexity and swap shortening for European-style butter in the craquelin.

instructions.step: Mix dough: flour, sugar, yeast, eggs, milk, vanilla and butter. Knead 10 min.
Proof 1.5 hours.
Shape into round buns. Make topping: shortening, sugar and cocoa (for chocolate) or vanilla.
Spread topping on buns, score in a shell pattern.
Bake at 180°C for 18 min.

name: Sopa Azteca
description: Tortilla soup with pasilla chillies, tomato broth, avocado and cheese. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use a gelatin-rich, house-made brown chicken stock to achieve a Michelin-level mouthfeel.

instructions.step: (Store-bought corn tortillas are standard and preferred — warm on a dry griddle. To make from scratch: mix 200g masa harina, ½ tsp salt, 240ml warm water; press into thin rounds and cook 1 min per side.)  Fry tortilla strips until crispy.
Fry torn pasilla chillies until puffed. Set aside.
Blend tomatoes, garlic and onion. Fry purée in oil 5 min.
Add broth. Simmer 15 min.
Serve with tortilla strips, fried chillies, avocado, cotija and crema.

name: Molcajete de Mariscos
description: Mixed seafood in a spicy tomato broth served in a stone mortar. ⭐ Pro Tip: Decrease seafood simmer time to three minutes to preserve texture and finish with fresh lime zest for acidity.

instructions.step: Make sauce: toast chillies, blend with tomatoes, garlic and chipotle.
Sear all seafood briefly.
Simmer seafood in sauce 8 min.
Pour into hot molcajete. Top with cheese.
Serve with warm tortillas.

name: Chiles en Nogada
description: Poblano stuffed with spiced fruit and pork in walnut cream - Mexico flag dish. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap cream cheese for aged goat cheese and add diced panochera apples to the picadillo for superior depth.

instructions.step: Char, steam, peel and deseed poblanos.
Cook pork with dried fruits, spices and sherry for filling.
Stuff peppers.
Make nogada: blend walnuts, cream cheese and sherry until smooth.
Dress peppers with nogada, pomegranate and parsley.

name: Tacos de Canasta
description: Basket tacos filled with refried beans, chicharron or potato in corn tortillas. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse layering oil with guajillo and garlic for superior aroma and color.

instructions.step: (Store-bought corn tortillas are standard and preferred — warm on a dry griddle. To make from scratch: mix 200g masa harina, ½ tsp salt, 240ml warm water; press into thin rounds and cook 1 min per side.)  Fill warm tortillas with desired filling.
Double the tortilla. Stack in a basket lined with plastic.
Close basket 15 min to steam.
Serve with salsa verde and pickled jalapenos.

name: Capirotada
description: Mexican Lenten bread pudding with piloncillo syrup, raisins and cheese. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the syrup with orange zest and star anise for depth.

instructions.step: Make syrup: simmer piloncillo with water and cinnamon until dissolved.
Toast bread slices. Layer in a greased baking dish.
Scatter raisins, peanuts and crumbled queso fresco between bread layers.
Pour syrup over. Bake at 180C for 20 min.

name: Consome de Pollo
description: Clear, golden chicken broth with avocado, rice and chipotle. ⭐ Pro Tip: Roast aromatics prior to simmering to achieve a dark gold hue and intensified umami.

instructions.step: Simmer chicken with vegetables 35 min. Strain and season broth.
Cook the rice: rinse 100g long-grain rice. Add to 200ml salted water, bring to a boil, cover and simmer 15 min until tender. Fluff with a fork.
Shred chicken.
Serve broth with rice, shredded chicken, avocado, chipotle and lime.

name: Bacalhau à Brás
description: Shredded salt cod scrambled with matchstick potatoes, eggs and olives. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with high-phenolic olive oil and salt-cured Galega olives to elevate the flavor profile.

instructions.step: Soak salt cod 48 hours. Shred finely.
Fry matchstick potatoes until golden. Drain.
Sauté onion and garlic. Add cod.
Add potatoes. Pour in beaten eggs and scramble off the heat.
Top with olives and parsley. Serve immediately.

name: Pastéis de Nata
description: Lisbon's legendary custard tarts with a flaky, caramelised burnt top. Store-bought puff pastry is standard — or make from scratch by folding butter into flour dough 6 times with resting periods between each turn. ⭐ Pro Tip: Utilize a baking stone to ensure the puff pastry base achieves a crisp, laminated shatter without grease soakage.

instructions.step: Roll puff pastry, cut circles and press into tart tins.
Make custard: whisk yolks with sugar and flour. Add hot milk and cream. Stir until thick.
Fill pastry shells ¾ full.
Bake at 250°C for 12–15 min until custard blisters and blackens slightly.
Dust with cinnamon. Serve warm.

name: Caldo Verde
description: Portugal's national soup — potato and kale broth with chouriço. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the chouriço pan with stock to incorporate those caramelized sugars back into the soup base.

instructions.step: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil.
Add diced potato and 1.2L water. Simmer 20 min.
Blend smooth. Return to pan.
Add finely shredded kale. Simmer 5 min.
Fry chouriço slices. Serve soup with chouriço and drizzle of oil.

name: Bacalhau à Lagareiro
description: Salt cod baked in the oven with smashed potatoes and pools of olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with fresh gremolata or lemon zest to elevate the dish's brightness and visual appeal.

instructions.step: Soak cod 48 hours. Pre-cook potatoes whole 20 min.
Smash potatoes flat. Season with salt.
Arrange cod and potatoes in a roasting tin. Add garlic and rosemary.
Pour generous olive oil over everything.
Bake at 200°C for 30 min.

name: Cataplana de Mariscos
description: Mixed shellfish steamed in the iconic copper cataplana with tomatoes and wine. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a splash of fresh lemon juice and cold extra virgin olive oil to brighten the heavy oceanic flavors.

instructions.step: Layer chouriço, onion, garlic, tomatoes in a cataplana or heavy pot.
Add all shellfish and wine.
Seal and cook on high heat 10–12 min until all shellfish are open.
Scatter coriander. Serve with crusty bread.

name: Frango no Churrasco
description: Portuguese piri piri chargrilled chicken — fiery, smoky and irresistible. ⭐ Pro Tip: Implement a dry-brine prior to marinating to maximize skin crispness.

instructions.step: Butterfly the chicken. Blend piri piri, garlic, lemon, oil and paprika to a paste.
Marinate chicken 2+ hours.
Grill over charcoal or high heat 25–30 min, turning often.
Rest 5 min.
Serve with piri piri sauce, chips and salad.

name: Arroz de Pato
description: Duck rice baked in the oven with chouriço and crispy top. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate fresh orange zest and finely chopped parsley as a final garnish to brighten the palate.

instructions.step: Confit duck legs in their own fat 1.5 hours.
Make rice in duck broth with wine and aromatics.
Shred duck. Mix with rice.
Spread in a baking dish. Top with chouriço slices.
Bake at 200°C for 20 min until crispy on top.

name: Açorda à Alentejana
description: Alentejo bread soup with garlic, coriander, olive oil and a poached egg. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with micro-coriander and a final drizzle of premium cold-pressed oil for a refined finish.

instructions.step: Pound garlic and coriander with salt in a mortar.
Bring water to boil. Add garlic paste.
Tear bread into bowl. Pour boiling broth over.
Poach eggs directly in the soup.
Drizzle with olive oil. Serve in the pot.

name: Bifanas
description: Pork cutlets in a garlicky wine sauce — Portugal's beloved street sandwich. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap loin for thin-sliced pork neck to improve tenderness and flavor retention.

instructions.step: Pound pork cutlets thin. Marinate in wine, garlic, paprika and bay 1 hour.
Cook in marinade 8–10 min until cooked through and sauce reduces.
Add mustard to sauce.
Serve pork in crusty rolls with piri piri sauce.

name: Sopa de Pedra
description: Stone soup — a rich bean and pork stew from Almeirim. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a splash of red wine vinegar and fresh mint to elevate the dish to restaurant standards.

instructions.step: Soak beans overnight. Simmer with pork ribs 40 min.
Add chouriço, blood sausage, diced vegetables and garlic.
Simmer 20 more min until everything is tender.
Season. Serve with cornbread.

name: Polvo à Lagareiro
description: Roasted octopus over smashed potatoes with a waterfall of olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a splash of white wine vinegar to the final drizzle for essential acidity to cut the heavy fat.

instructions.step: Simmer octopus 40 min until tender.
Parboil potatoes. Smash flat on a baking tray.
Place octopus and potatoes in oven at 220°C for 15 min.
Drizzle generously with olive oil and add garlic and rosemary.

name: Toucinho do Céu
description: Heavenly almond and egg yolk pudding cake from the convents of Portugal. ⭐ Pro Tip: Bake in a bain-marie to achieve a Michelin-standard custardy crumb rather than a dry cake finish.

instructions.step: Make a heavy syrup with sugar and water, cook to thread stage.
Beat yolks. Add hot syrup slowly, whisking constantly.
Fold in almonds, butter, cinnamon and lemon zest.
Pour into a greased tin. Bake at 180°C for 25 min.
Cool before slicing.

name: Caldeirada de Peixe
description: Portuguese layered fish stew with potato, tomato and white wine. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute wine with a concentrated fish stock and add pimentão for smoky complexity.

instructions.step: Layer raw sliced potato, onion, tomato and garlic in a pot.
Add fish on top. Add wine, olive oil and bay leaves.
Cover and cook on medium-low 25 min without stirring.
Scatter parsley. Serve with crusty bread.

name: Pão de Ló
description: Light and airy Portuguese sponge cake — the ancestor of all European sponges. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the sugar with lemon zest overnight to maximize aromatic intensity before beating.

instructions.step: Beat whole eggs with sugar and lemon zest for 15 min until very thick and pale.
Sift in flour. Fold very gently.
Pour into a greased tin.
Bake at 180°C for 18 min — center should remain slightly wobbly.

name: Amêijoas à Bulhão Pato
description: Clams steamed with white wine, garlic, lemon and a handful of coriander. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace standard white wine with a crisp Vinho Verde to provide the necessary high acidity that cuts through the olive oil.

instructions.step: Heat olive oil with garlic until golden.
Add clams and wine. Cover and cook 4–5 min until all open.
Discard any closed clams.
Squeeze lemon over, scatter coriander. Serve with bread.

name: Febras de Porco Grelhadas
description: Thin grilled pork steaks marinated in wine, garlic and paprika. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a smoked paprika-infused oil and fleur de sel to bridge the gap between home cooking and fine dining.

instructions.step: Marinate pork in wine, garlic, paprika, olive oil and bay leaf 1 hour.
