Grill on high heat 4–5 min per side.
Squeeze lemon over. Scatter parsley.
Serve with chips and salad.

name: Arroz Doce
description: Portuguese creamy rice pudding decorated with cinnamon in traditional patterns. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the milk with whole cinnamon sticks and lemon peel instead of just zest for a cleaner, more sophisticated flavor.

instructions.step: Cook rice in water 10 min. Drain.
Add milk and lemon zest. Simmer on low heat 20 min, stirring frequently, until the rice absorbs the milk and the mixture becomes thick and velvety.
Add sugar and butter. Stir 5 min.
Remove from heat. Quickly stir in egg yolks.
Pour into dish. Cool and decorate with cinnamon patterns.

name: Rojões à Moda do Minho
description: Minho-style pork cubes with vinho verde and chestnuts. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the pan with a cold splash of Vinho Verde after frying to emulsify the fat into a glossy sauce.

instructions.step: Cube pork and marinate in vinho verde, garlic, paprika, bay leaf and cumin for 2 hours.
Cook in marinade until liquid evaporates and pork fries in its own fat.
Add peeled chestnuts. Cook 15 min.
Squeeze lemon over. Serve with cornbread.

name: Bacalhau com Natas
description: Salt cod layered with cream, potatoes and baked until golden. ⭐ Pro Tip: Fold in fresh parsley and lemon zest to provide essential acidity.

instructions.step: Make béchamel: melt 40g butter in a saucepan, whisk in 40g flour, cook 1 min. Gradually add 400ml hot milk, whisking constantly until thick and smooth. Season with nutmeg and salt.
Soak cod 48 hours. Poach and flake.
Fry potato strips until golden. Sauté onion and garlic.
Layer cod, potato and onion in baking dish.
Mix cream with béchamel. Pour over.
Top with Parmesan. Bake at 190°C for 25 min.

name: Sericaia
description: Alentejo soufflé-style egg and cinnamon pudding with plums in syrup. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap milk for half-and-half and use fresh Elvas plums poached in Star Anise and Port wine for depth.

instructions.step: Whisk egg yolks with sugar until pale. Add flour, milk and lemon zest.
Whip whites to stiff peaks.
Fold whites into yolk mixture.
Pour into a baking dish. Scatter cinnamon.
Bake at 180°C for 20–25 min until puffed.
Serve warm with plums.

name: Sardinhas Assadas
description: Grilled whole fresh sardines with olive oil and lemon — the taste of Lisbon in June. ⭐ Pro Tip: Grate lemon zest over the fish post-grill for aromatic lift and serve with a charred green pepper salad for acidity.

instructions.step: Season sardines generously with salt.
Grill over charcoal or high heat 3–4 min per side.
Serve immediately with olive oil, lemon and parsley.
Eat with cornbread to soak up the juices.

name: Feijoada à Portuguesa
description: Portuguese white bean stew with pork, chouriço and seasonal vegetables. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze with Vinho Verde and finish with a bright acid like lemon juice to cut the richness.

instructions.step: Soak beans overnight. Drain.
Brown pork and chouriço. Sauté onion, garlic and carrot.
Add beans, tomatoes, bay leaves and water to cover.
Simmer 60 min until beans are very soft.
Add kale for final 5 min.

name: Queijadas de Sintra
description: Small fresh cheese and cinnamon tarts from the royal village of Sintra. ⭐ Pro Tip: Reduce sugar by 15% and incorporate a pinch of sea salt to elevate the cheese's lactic notes.

instructions.step: Blend cheese, sugar and butter until smooth.
Add eggs one by one. Fold in flour.
Add cinnamon and lemon zest.
Pour into greased tart moulds.
Bake at 190°C for 18 min until golden.

name: Migas Alentejanas
description: Garlicky fried breadcrumbs with coriander — served alongside grilled meats. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a finish of high-quality sea salt and a micro-coriander garnish to elevate the presentation.

instructions.step: Tear bread into small pieces.
Fry garlic in olive oil until golden.
Add bread and a splash of water. Fry, stirring, until bread is crispy and golden.
Add coriander, season and serve alongside grilled pork or bacalhau.

name: Papas de Sarrabulho
description: Minho-style blood and offal porridge with bread — robust and historical. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the broth with cloves and cinnamon to balance the earthy iron notes.

instructions.step: Cook offal with onion, garlic, wine and cumin until tender.
Remove and shred. Reserve broth.
Add torn bread to broth. Simmer until porridge consistency.
Slowly add blood, stirring. Do not boil.
Return offal. Finish with lemon juice.

name: Acorda de Marisco
description: Alentejo bread soup with clams, shrimp and coriander. ⭐ Pro Tip: Emulsify with a high-phenolic finishing oil and deglaze with an acidic Vinho Verde for brightness.

instructions.step: Steam clams and shrimp open in wine. Reserve broth.
Pound garlic and coriander with salt. Fry in olive oil.
Add broth. Bring to boil.
Tear bread into pot. Stir until dissolved.
Add seafood. Crack eggs in. Cover 2 min.

name: Iscas com Elas
description: Lisbon liver and onions - marinated in white wine and vinegar with potatoes. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the pan with the marinade and mount with cold butter for a glossy, emulsified jus.

instructions.step: Marinate liver slices in wine, vinegar, garlic and bay overnight.
Boil potatoes. Slice.
Fry onion rings in oil until soft.
Quickly fry liver in same pan 1 min per side.
Serve with fried potatoes and onion.

name: Caldo de Grao
description: Chickpea broth with spinach and boiled egg - a simple Portuguese staple. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace hard-boiled eggs with soft-poached or 63-degree eggs to provide a rich, natural emulsion for the broth.

instructions.step: Simmer chickpeas, carrot and potato in water 25 min.
Add spinach. Cook 5 min.
Season.
Hard boil eggs separately. Serve soup with egg halves and olive oil drizzle.

name: Bola de Carne
description: Portuguese bread stuffed with chorizo, linguica and cured meats. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce a 20% rye or whole-grain blend to the dough for earthy complexity and increased fiber.

instructions.step: Make the dough: combine 500g bread flour, 7g yeast, 1 tsp salt, 300ml warm water, 2 tbsp olive oil. Knead 8 min. Rest 1 hr until doubled. Or use store-bought bread dough.
Roll dough into a large rectangle.
Layer sliced meats over the dough.
Roll tightly into a log. Seal ends.
Proof 45 min.
Brush with egg wash. Bake at 190C for 40 min.

name: Mousse de Chocolate
description: Rich, airy chocolate mousse made with egg whites and dark chocolate. ⭐ Pro Tip: Upgrade to a high-fat cultured butter and finish with a citrus zest or cocoa nibs for textural contrast.

instructions.step: Melt chocolate with butter and coffee.
Beat yolks with sugar until pale. Fold in chocolate.
Whip whites to stiff peaks. Fold into chocolate in three stages.
Chill 4 hours.

name: Chicken Adobo
description: Chicken braised in vinegar, soy sauce and garlic — the Filipino national dish. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the pan with a splash of dry sherry for added complexity.

instructions.step: Marinate chicken in soy, vinegar, garlic, bay and pepper 30 min.
Sear chicken in oil until browned.
Return marinade. Simmer covered 25 min.
Uncover and reduce sauce until thick.
Serve with steamed white rice.

name: Sinigang na Baboy
description: Pork ribs in a sour tamarind broth with kangkong and radish. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with cold-pressed calamansi juice to elevate the acidity above standard paste.

instructions.step: Simmer ribs with tomatoes and onion 25 min.
Add tamarind, daikon and eggplant. Simmer 10 min.
Add kangkong and chilli. Cook 3 min.
Season with fish sauce. Serve with rice.

name: Kare-Kare
description: Oxtail stew in a rich peanut sauce served with bagoong shrimp paste. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate house-made roasted peanut paste and toasted rice flour for authentic depth and texture.

instructions.step: Simmer oxtail until tender, 1.5 hours.
Sauté onion and garlic with annatto oil.
Add oxtail and broth. Stir in peanut butter until smooth.
Add vegetables. Simmer 10 min.
Serve with bagoong on the side.

name: Lechon Kawali
description: Deep-fried pork belly with shatteringly crispy skin. ⭐ Pro Tip: Pair with a sharp, acidic atchara or calamansi dipping sauce to chemically balance the heavy fats.

instructions.step: Boil pork belly with garlic, bay, peppercorns and salt until tender, 45 min.
Pat very dry. Refrigerate uncovered overnight to dry skin.
Deep fry at 190°C for 10–12 min until skin is blistered and crackly.
Chop into pieces. Serve with lechon sauce.

name: Bistek Tagalog
description: Beef sirloin in a soy and calamansi sauce with rings of caramelised onion. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish the sauce with a cold butter mount to achieve a glossy, Michelin-standard 'velouté' consistency.

instructions.step: Marinate beef slices in soy, calamansi, garlic and pepper 30 min.
Sauté onion rings in oil until soft and golden. Set aside.
Fry beef over high heat 2 min per side.
Pour marinade over, cook 2 min.
Top with caramelised onion rings. Serve with rice.

name: Pancit Bihon
description: Stir-fried rice vermicelli with vegetables, chicken and pork. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with crispy garlic chips and a splash of high-quality sesame oil for a five-star mouthfeel.

instructions.step: Soak vermicelli in water 15 min. Drain.
Stir-fry pork and chicken until cooked. Set aside.
Fry garlic and onion. Add vegetables.
Add noodles and broth. Toss until absorbed.
Return meat. Add soy sauce. Toss and serve with calamansi.

name: Tinola
description: Light ginger chicken soup with green papaya and chilli leaves. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the cooking oil with ginger before sautéing to intensify the base flavor profile.

instructions.step: Sauté garlic, onion and ginger until fragrant.
Add chicken, brown lightly.
Add broth and fish sauce. Simmer 20 min.
Add papaya. Cook 8 min.
Add malunggay. Cook 2 min.
Serve with rice.

name: Adobong Kangkong
description: Water spinach stir-fried with garlic, vinegar and soy — a Filipino classic side. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the fried garlic with the liquids to create an emulsion before folding in the greens.

instructions.step: Fry garlic in oil until golden.
Add kangkong. Stir-fry 2 min.
Add soy sauce and vinegar.
Toss briefly. Serve immediately with rice.

name: Laing
description: Dried taro leaves slow-cooked in coconut milk with chilli — smoky and rich. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use cured pork belly for complex salinity and finish with a 'kakang gata' coconut cream reduction for a glossy, five-star sheen.

instructions.step: Do not stir the leaves — place dried taro directly in coconut milk.
Add pork, garlic, ginger, chilli and shrimp paste.
Cook on medium heat 40 min without stirring.
Add fish sauce. Stir for the first time once leaves are tender.
Serve with rice.

name: Sisig
description: Sizzling chopped pork face and ears with onion, chilli and egg on a hot plate. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the sizzling plate with a splash of premium dry sherry to add complexity to the fatty acids.

instructions.step: Boil and grill pork until crispy. Chop into small pieces.
Mix with onion, chilli, soy, calamansi and mayo.
Heat a cast iron plate until screaming hot.
Add butter, then sisig. Crack an egg on top.
Serve sizzling. Stir egg in at the table.

name: Nilaga
description: Simple boiled beef and vegetable soup with peppercorns and fish sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Blanch the beef first for a crystal-clear broth and incorporate charred onion for smoky depth.

instructions.step: Simmer beef shank with onion and peppercorns 40 min.
Add potato and carrot. Cook 10 min.
Add cabbage. Cook 3 min.
Season with fish sauce. Serve in bowls with scallion.

name: Pork Sinigang
description: Pork belly in a sour tamarind broth with assorted vegetables. ⭐ Pro Tip: Sear the pork first to develop Maillard depth and introduce a chilled ice-bath shock for the greens to maintain vibrant color.

instructions.step: Simmer pork belly with tomatoes and onion 30 min.
Add tamarind. Taste and adjust sourness.
Add eggplant, okra and long beans. Cook 10 min.
Season with fish sauce. Serve with rice.

name: Bulalo
description: Beef marrow shank soup boiled until the fat renders into a rich, clear broth. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate charred aromatics like ginger and onion during the simmer to enhance the flavor profile.

instructions.step: Blanch beef 5 min. Drain and rinse.
Cover with fresh water. Boil then simmer 2 hours.
Add corn. Cook 15 min.
Add cabbage. Cook 5 min.
Season with fish sauce. Serve in bowls.

name: Tortang Talong
description: Charred whole eggplant dipped in egg and pan-fried — a Filipino breakfast staple. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap commercial ketchup for a signature calamansi-honey reduction and finish with sea salt flakes to provide a sophisticated acidity-salt...

instructions.step: Char eggplant over gas flame until blackened. Peel.
Flatten each eggplant keeping stem intact.
Beat eggs with fish sauce and garlic.
Dip eggplant in egg. Fry in oil 3 min per side until set.
Serve with steamed rice and banana ketchup.

name: Pinakbet
description: Ilocano mixed vegetable stew with shrimp paste and bitter melon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate crispy pork belly for a 5-star mouthfeel and deglaze with a refined shrimp fumet.

instructions.step: Sauté garlic, onion and tomatoes until soft.
Add bagoong. Cook 1 min.
Add vegetables in order of cooking time: squash first, then eggplant, okra, long beans, bitter melon.
Cook covered 12 min.
Serve with rice.

name: Chicken Inasal
description: Bacolod-style marinated chicken grilled over charcoal with annatto oil baste. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap cane vinegar for calamansi juice and add 15g of muscovado sugar to the marinade for superior caramelization.

instructions.step: Marinate chicken with vinegar, lemongrass, garlic, ginger, salt and pepper 2 hours.
Grill over charcoal, basting with annatto oil every few minutes, 25 min.
Serve with rice, garlic vinegar and annatto oil.

name: Bicol Express
description: Pork slow-simmered in coconut milk with loads of green and red chillies. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with fresh coconut cream at the very end to enhance the velvety mouthfeel.

instructions.step: Sauté garlic, ginger and onion.
Add pork. Brown lightly.
Add shrimp paste, chillies and coconut milk.
Simmer uncovered 20 min until sauce thickens.
Serve with rice.

name: Pancit Canton
description: Thick egg noodles stir-fried with pork, shrimp and mixed vegetables. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze with Shaoxing wine and finish with white pepper and toasted sesame oil for a sophisticated aromatic profile.

instructions.step: Stir-fry pork and shrimp. Set aside.
Fry garlic. Add vegetables.
Add noodles, broth, soy and oyster sauce.
Toss until noodles are tender and liquid absorbed.
Return meat. Serve with calamansi.

name: Halo-Halo
description: Filipino shaved ice dessert with beans, jelly, ube, flan and evaporated milk. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace evaporated milk with infused carabao milk and incorporate a sea salt element to balance the heavy sugar profile.

instructions.step: Layer beans, jelly, corn and kaong in tall glasses.
Pack with shaved ice.
Top with ube ice cream and leche flan.
Pour evaporated milk over.
Mix everything together — that's halo-halo (mix mix)!

name: Ginataang Manok
description: Chicken braised in coconut milk with ginger, chilli and lemongrass. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate kaffir lime leaves for aromatic depth and finish with a bright splash of calamansi to cut through the heavy lipid profile.

instructions.step: Sauté garlic, ginger and onion.
Add chicken. Brown lightly.
Add coconut milk, lemongrass and chilli.
Simmer 25 min until chicken is tender and sauce has thickened.
Season with fish sauce. Serve with rice.

name: Arroz Caldo
description: Filipino ginger rice porridge with chicken, boiled egg and fried garlic. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with crispy chicken skin cracklings and a dash of white pepper to elevate the dish to a 5-star level.

instructions.step: Sauté garlic until golden. Reserve half for topping.
Add ginger and chicken. Cook 5 min.
Add rice and broth. Simmer 30 min stirring often.
Boil eggs separately.
Serve topped with fried garlic, scallion, halved egg and calamansi.

name: Tokwa't Baboy
description: Fried firm tofu and pork ears in a sweet-sour vinegar and soy dipping sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate charred shallots and ginger into the dressing for a refined aromatic layer.

instructions.step: Boil pork ears until tender. Slice thin.
Deep fry tofu until golden and crispy.
Mix soy, vinegar, sugar, onion and chilli for dipping sauce.
Slice tofu. Arrange with pork ears on a plate.
Serve with dipping sauce.

name: Ginataan
description: Coconut milk dessert soup with sweet potato, bananas, sago and jackfruit. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the coconut milk with fresh pandan leaves and sea salt to elevate the flavor profile.

instructions.step: Cook sago pearls in water until translucent, 15 min. Drain.
Simmer coconut milk with sugar.
Add sweet potato, cook 8 min.
Add banana, jackfruit and sago. Simmer 5 min.
Serve warm or chilled.

name: Pork Lumpia Shanghai
description: Crispy deep-fried spring rolls filled with seasoned ground pork. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate minced shrimp for a luxurious flavor profile and replace the sweet chili sauce with a house-made calamansi-vinegar dip.

instructions.step: (Use store-bought spring roll wrappers. Scratch wrappers: mix 200g flour, 1 egg, 250ml water into thin batter, cook paper-thin crepes on a non-stick pan.) Mix pork with grated carrot, onion, garlic, soy and egg.
Place 1 tbsp filling on each wrapper. Roll tight and seal with egg white.
Deep fry at 175°C for 4–5 min until golden.
Drain. Serve with sweet chilli sauce.

name: Dinuguan
description: Filipino pork blood stew - savory, tangy and deeply flavorful. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze with a rich pork bone stock to enhance umami and depth.

instructions.step: Brown pork belly and offal.
Add garlic and onion.
Add vinegar and fish sauce. Do not stir for 2 min.
Add blood slowly, stirring.
Simmer 25 min. Add chilli.
Serve with puto (rice cake) or rice.

name: Bringhe
description: Pampanga sticky rice paella cooked in coconut milk with chicken. ⭐ Pro Tip: Integrate ginger and lemongrass into the base saute for aromatic lift and replace turmeric with saffron for a more sophisticated palate.

instructions.step: Saute chicken with garlic and onion.
Add rice, turmeric and coconut milk.
Cook covered stirring often 30 min until rice is set.
Press into banana leaf lined pan. Cook bottom until crispy.

name: Pakbet
description: Ilocano vegetable stew with bagoong and bitter melon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add crisped pork belly for textural contrast and deglaze with a shrimp-head reduction for Michelin-level umami.

instructions.step: Fry garlic, onion and tomatoes.
Add bagoong. Cook 1 min.
Add squash and eggplant. Cook 8 min.
Add okra and bitter melon. Cook 5 min.

name: Bibingka
description: Christmas rice cake baked in banana leaves with salted egg and coconut. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate Kesong Puti (native buffalo cheese) to add a sharp, salty contrast to the sugar.

instructions.step: Mix rice flour, sugar, baking powder, eggs and coconut milk.
Pour into banana leaf-lined moulds.
Top with sliced salted egg.
Bake at 200C for 15 min.
Brush with butter. Top with grated coconut.

name: Lumpiang Sariwa
description: Fresh Filipino spring rolls with lettuce wrap and sweet peanut sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Elevate the sauce with toasted galangal and fresh tamarind to balance the richness of the peanuts.

instructions.step: Fry tofu. Stir-fry carrot, cabbage and bean sprouts.
Season with soy sauce.
Place filling in lettuce leaf wrap.
Drizzle peanut sauce and crushed peanuts over.
Wrap and eat.

name: Champorado
description: Sweet chocolate rice porridge - a Filipino childhood classic. ⭐ Pro Tip: Upgrade to stone-ground cacao tablets and finish with a pinch of sea salt to elevate the profile.

instructions.step: Cook glutinous rice in water 15 min, stirring.
Add cocoa powder and sugar. Stir well.
Simmer 5 more min.
Drizzle coconut milk and condensed milk over.
Serve with fried tuyo on the side.

name: Beef Pho
description: Fragrant star anise and charred ginger beef broth with tender noodles. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate thinly sliced raw eye of round to cook instantly in the bowl for a premium finish.

instructions.step: Char ginger and onion over open flame.
Boil bones 10 min. Rinse. Simmer fresh with charred aromatics and spices 3 hours.
Strain broth. Season with fish sauce.
Cook noodles. Slice brisket thin.
Build bowls: noodles, brisket, hot broth. Serve with bean sprouts, basil, lime, hoisin and sriracha.

name: Bánh Mì Sandwich
description: Vietnamese baguette with pâté, char siu pork, pickled daikon and jalapeño. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate Maggi Seasoning for umami and replace standard mayonnaise with a rich, house-made yolk-based spread.

instructions.step: (For the char siu pork: find the full Char Siu Pork recipe in the Chinese catalog — marinate and roast the day before, or buy from a Chinese barbecue shop.) Quick-pickle daikon and carrot in vinegar and sugar 20 min.
Warm pork and split baguette.
Spread pâté on bottom, mayo and sriracha on top.
Fill with pork, pickled vegetables, cucumber, jalapeño and cilantro.

name: Fresh Spring Rolls (Gỏi Cuốn)
description: Rice paper rolls filled with shrimp, vermicelli, lettuce and fresh herbs. ⭐ Pro Tip: Poach shrimp in a ginger-lemongrass court-bouillon and brighten the sauce with fresh lime to balance the heavy nut profile.

instructions.step: Cook vermicelli. Peel and halve shrimp.
Soak rice paper one at a time in warm water 10 sec.
Lay on a damp cloth. Layer lettuce, vermicelli, shrimp, vegetables and herbs.
Roll tightly like a burrito.
Serve with peanut dipping sauce.

name: Bún Bò Huế
description: Central Vietnamese spicy lemongrass and shrimp paste beef broth with thick noodles. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add Vietnamese ham (chả lụa) and a touch of rock sugar to balance the shrimp paste's pungent salinity.

instructions.step: Simmer pork hock and beef shank 1.5 hours.
Make spice paste: blend lemongrass, shrimp paste and chilli.
Sauté paste in oil. Add to broth.
Season with fish sauce.

Cook round rice noodles: bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add noodles, cook 5–7 min until tender. Drain, rinse under cold water. Divide into bowls, ladle hot broth over, top with meat, bean sprouts, banana blossom and a squeeze of lime.

name: Cơm Tấm
description: Broken rice with grilled pork chop, egg meatloaf, fried egg and fish sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate wood ear mushrooms for textural contrast and finish with mỡ hành (scallion oil) for aromatic moisture.

instructions.step: (Broken rice (tấm) is the grains that shatter during milling — find it at Asian grocers. Cook like regular rice: rinse, add 1:1.25 water ratio, simmer covered 15 min.) Marinate pork chops in fish sauce, garlic, lemongrass and sugar 1 hour.
Grill pork chops 5 min per side.
Make egg meatloaf: steam beaten eggs with pork mince.
Serve broken rice with chop, meatloaf, fried egg, cucumber and tomato.

name: Bún Chả
description: Grilled pork patties and belly in a sweet-sour dipping broth with vermicelli. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate minced shallots and lemongrass into the meat to boost aromatic profiles.

instructions.step: Form pork mince into small patties. Marinate pork belly.
Grill both over charcoal until charred.
Make dipping broth: dilute fish sauce and sugar with warm water. Add garlic, lime and chilli.
Cook vermicelli. Serve with broth, grilled pork, fresh herbs and lettuce.

name: Bánh Xèo
description: Sizzling turmeric rice flour crêpe filled with pork, shrimp and bean sprouts. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use a seasoned carbon steel pan to maximize heat retention and ensure a clean, crisp release.

instructions.step: Make batter: mix rice flour, coconut milk, water and turmeric.
Heat oil in a wide pan. Stir-fry pork and shrimp until cooked through, 3 min.
Pour a thin ladle of batter. Add bean sprouts. Cover and cook 3 min until edges are lacy and crisp.
Fold like an omelette. Repeat for remaining batter, about 4–5 crepes total. Serve each wrapped in lettuce leaves with fresh mint and nuoc cham.

name: Gỏi Cuốn Chay (Vegan Rolls)
description: Rice paper rolls with avocado, mango, tofu and herbs. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate pickled daikon to provide a necessary acidic counterpoint to the fatty avocado and fried tofu.

instructions.step: Pan-fry tofu until golden.
Julienne carrot and mango.
Soak rice paper one at a time, fill with all ingredients, roll tight.
Serve with hoisin-peanut dipping sauce.

name: Chả Giò
description: Crispy fried Vietnamese spring rolls with pork, glass noodles and mushrooms. ⭐ Pro Tip: Refine the nuoc cham with hand-pounded garlic and bird's eye chili to ensure a sharp, bright finish.

instructions.step: (Use store-bought spring roll wrappers. Scratch wrappers: whisk 150g rice flour, 1 egg, 300ml water; cook paper-thin on a non-stick pan.) Soak noodles and mushrooms 10 min. Drain and chop.
Mix with pork, carrot, onion, egg and fish sauce.
Place 1 tbsp filling near the edge of each wrapper. Roll tightly tucking sides in. Seal the tip with egg white. Repeat — makes about 20 rolls.
Fry at 175°C for 4 min until golden.
Serve with nuoc cham.

name: Bò Lúc Lắc
description: Shaking beef — wok-seared tenderloin cubes with lime pepper salt dipping sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a knob of unsalted butter for a glossy, Michelin-style emulsion.

instructions.step: Cube beef. Marinate with soy, oyster sauce, garlic and sugar 20 min.
Sear in a very hot wok in batches, shaking the pan, 2 min.
Make dipping sauce: lime juice and cracked black pepper.
Serve beef on watercress with red onion. Dip each bite in lime pepper sauce.

name: Phở Gà
description: Clear, delicate chicken pho with ginger and star anise. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate toasted coriander seeds and a pinch of rock sugar for balanced salinity and depth.

instructions.step: Char ginger and onion. Simmer chicken with aromatics 50 min.
Remove chicken, shred. Strain and season broth with fish sauce.
Cook noodles.
Build bowls: noodles, shredded chicken, hot broth.
Serve with bean sprouts, basil and lime.

name: Canh Chua
description: Vietnamese sweet and sour soup with fish, pineapple and tomato. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce fried garlic and rice paddy herb (ngo om) at the finish for authentic 5-star complexity.

instructions.step: Simmer tamarind in water. Add tomatoes and pineapple.
Add fish. Cook 8 min.
Add bean sprouts, okra and elephant ear stem.
Season with fish sauce and sugar.
Top with coriander.

name: Bún Thịt Nướng
description: Grilled pork over rice vermicelli with fresh herbs, pickled vegetables and nuoc cham. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce toasted peanuts and crispy shallots to provide critical textural contrast and healthy lipid profiles.

instructions.step: Marinate pork with lemongrass, fish sauce, sugar and garlic 1 hour.
Grill pork until charred and cooked through. Slice thin.
Quick-pickle daikon and carrot.
Serve in bowls: noodles, lettuce, pork, pickles, fresh herbs, nuoc cham.

name: Xôi Gà
description: Sticky rice steamed with pandan, topped with shredded chicken and fried shallots. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace breast with chicken thigh for improved mouthfeel and glaze the rice with a chicken fat-shallot oil infusion.

instructions.step: Soak glutinous rice 4 hours. Steam with coconut milk and pandan 25 min.
Poach chicken. Shred and season with fish sauce.
Serve rice topped with chicken, fried shallots and sesame seeds.

name: Bánh Cuốn
description: Steamed rice paper rolls with minced pork, wood ear mushrooms and fried shallots. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace twenty percent of the rice flour with tapioca starch for superior translucency and chew.

instructions.step: Make thin batter with rice flour and water.
Cook pork with mushrooms and shallots. Season with fish sauce.
Steam batter in a thin layer on a cloth. Add filling and roll.
Serve with nuoc cham, cucumber and fried shallots.

name: Cà Phê Trứng
description: Hanoi egg coffee — strong robusta with a creamy beaten egg yolk foam. ⭐ Pro Tip: Serve the cup in a warm water bath to maintain foam temperature and prevent the egg from setting.

instructions.step: Brew strong Vietnamese coffee using a phin filter.
Beat egg yolks with condensed milk and sugar until pale and thick.
Pour coffee into a small cup.
Spoon the whipped egg yolk and condensed milk mixture generously on top of the hot coffee.
Serve immediately — drink through the foam or stir together.

name: Bánh Flan (Crème Caramel)
description: Vietnamese-French crème caramel — richer and darker than the French original. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace two whole eggs with four yolks for a richer, melt-in-the-mouth consistency.

instructions.step: Make caramel with sugar and water. Pour into ramekins.
Whisk eggs with condensed milk, whole milk, vanilla and coffee.
Strain into ramekins.
Steam on low heat 35 min or bake in water bath.
Chill 4 hours. Unmould and serve.

name: Gỏi Ngó Sen
description: Lotus root salad with shrimp, pork, fresh herbs and nuoc cham dressing. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a drop of bird's eye chili oil to the dressing for nuanced heat and controlled gloss.

instructions.step: Slice lotus root thin. Blanch 2 min. Chill.
Poach shrimp and pork belly. Slice.
Julienne carrot.
Toss everything with nuoc cham.
Top with fried shallots and peanuts.

name: Mì Quảng
description: Central Vietnamese turmeric noodles with pork, shrimp and crispy rice crackers. ⭐ Pro Tip: Bloom turmeric and shallots in oil before deglazing to intensify the broth's aromatic profile.

instructions.step: Simmer pork ribs until tender. Add turmeric and fish sauce to broth.
Add shrimp for last 5 min.
Blanch noodles briefly.
Serve noodles with a little broth, pork, shrimp, banana flower, sprouts, peanuts and crackers.

name: Thịt Kho
description: Caramelised pork belly and eggs braised in coconut water. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with fresh chili and a squeeze of lime to provide the acidity necessary to cut through the intense fat.

instructions.step: Caramelise sugar until amber. Add pork, turning to coat.
Add coconut water, fish sauce, garlic and shallots.
Simmer covered 40 min.
Hard boil eggs separately. Peel and add to braise.
Simmer uncovered 15 min to glaze.
Serve with rice and pickled mustard greens.

name: Chè Ba Màu
description: Three color dessert with red bean, pandan jelly and mung bean over crushed ice. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the coconut milk with sea salt and pandan leaf to create a sophisticated flavor balance.

instructions.step: Cook red beans and mung beans with sugar separately until soft.
Make pandan jelly from agar and pandan water. Set and cut into cubes.
Layer in a tall glass: red bean, pandan jelly, mung bean.
Pack with crushed ice.
Pour coconut milk over. Serve immediately.

name: Bún Riêu Cua
description: Crab and tomato broth with rice noodles, tofu and fresh herbs. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce annatto seed oil for a signature red hue and include mắm tôm to provide the necessary funky backbone.

instructions.step: Simmer pork ribs 30 min.
Add tamarind, tomatoes and fish sauce.
Mix crab paste with egg, drop into simmering broth.
Add tofu.
Cook noodles. Serve with bean sprouts and herbs.

name: Nem Nướng
description: Grilled Vietnamese pork sausage skewers served in rice paper wraps. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute half the ground pork with gio song (pork paste) for elite texture.

instructions.step: Mix pork with garlic, fish sauce, sugar and five spice.
Shape onto skewers. Grill 8–10 min, turning.
Serve with rice paper, lettuce, vermicelli and dipping sauce.
Wrap and dip each bite.

name: Súp Bắp Cua
description: Vietnamese crab and corn egg drop soup — silky, sweet and savory. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce a splash of premium sesame oil and white pepper at the finish to elevate the olfactory experience.

instructions.step: Bring broth to boil. Add corn kernels.
Thicken with cornstarch slurry.
Add crab meat. Simmer 3 min.
Drizzle beaten egg in a thin stream, stirring gently.
Season with fish sauce. Serve topped with scallion and coriander.

name: Bun Ken
description: Southern Vietnamese coconut fish curry noodle soup. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute half the coconut milk with fish stock to balance macros and highlight the seafood.

instructions.step: Saute lemongrass and galangal. Add turmeric.
Add fish and coconut milk. Simmer 15 min.
Add pineapple and fish sauce.
Cook rice vermicelli: soak in cold water 10 min, then drain and cook in boiling water 2–3 min until just tender. Drain, rinse under cold water, divide into bowls and ladle the curry broth over.
Serve with bean sprouts and lime.

name: Cha Ca La Vong
description: Hanoi turmeric and dill fish with rice noodles and peanuts. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce a charcoal-grilled sear before the final oil finish to replicate 5-star authenticity.

instructions.step: Marinate fish with turmeric, galangal and shrimp paste 30 min.
Pan-fry fish in oil until golden, 3 min per side.
Add dill and scallion, toss briefly.
Serve over vermicelli with peanuts and nuoc cham.

name: Xoi Xeo
description: Sticky rice topped with yellow mung bean paste and fried shallots. ⭐ Pro Tip: Fry shallots in duck fat for an umami boost and incorporate a splash of pandan leaf water during rice steaming for aromatic complexity.

instructions.step: Soak glutinous rice 4 hours. Steam with turmeric 25 min.
Soak mung beans 2 hours. Steam until soft. Mash with coconut milk.
Layer rice and mung bean paste.
Top with fried shallots and sesame oil.

name: Banh Beo
description: Steamed water rice cakes topped with dried shrimp and fried shallots. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce scallion oil for lipid-based flavor transport and crispy pork skin for textural contrast.

instructions.step: Mix rice flour and tapioca with water to form a thin batter.
Pour into small shallow dishes. Steam 5 min until set.
Pound dried shrimp coarsely.
Top cakes with dried shrimp and fried shallots.
Make dipping sauce with fish sauce, sugar and lime.

name: Banh Khot
description: Mini crispy coconut rice cups topped with shrimp. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute a portion of liquid with beer or seltzer for superior aeration and crunch.

instructions.step: Make batter: rice flour, coconut milk, turmeric and salt.
Heat a khot (or aebleskiver) pan over medium heat. Oil each cup well, 2 min.
Fill each cup halfway with batter. Press a shrimp into each. Cover and cook 3 min until edges are set and base is golden. Cook in batches.
Serve in lettuce with mint and nuoc cham.

name: Com Chien Duong Chau
description: Vietnamese Yangzhou fried rice with char siu and shrimp. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate diced Lap Cheong and minced shallots for authentic depth and a luxury mouthfeel.

instructions.step: (Use leftover char siu pork from the Chinese Char Siu Pork recipe, or buy from a Chinese barbecue shop. Day-old jasmine rice works best — cook 300g rice, spread on a tray and refrigerate overnight.) Scramble eggs in hot wok. Set aside.
Stir-fry shrimp and pork.
Add rice. Fry on high heat 3 min.
Add peas, sauces and eggs. Toss.
Finish with scallion and sesame oil.

name: Mezze Platter
description: A feast of hummus, baba ganoush, tabbouleh, falafel, pita and olives. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a signature house-made sauce like zhug or harissa to add acidic contrast.

instructions.step: Arrange hummus and baba ganoush in bowls.
Slice cucumber and tomatoes.
Warm pita and arrange alongside.
Place all items on a large board with olives and stuffed leaves.
Drizzle olive oil over hummus. Serve immediately.

name: Shish Tawook
description: Lebanese garlic-marinated chicken skewers with toum and pickles. ⭐ Pro Tip: Transition to metal skewers to conduct heat internally and prevent overcooking the exterior.

instructions.step: Marinate chicken with yogurt, lemon, garlic and spices 2+ hours.
Thread onto skewers. Grill over high heat 10–12 min.
Serve in pita with toum garlic sauce, tomatoes and pickles.

name: Hummus Masabacha
description: Warm whole chickpea hummus topped with olive oil, cumin and paprika. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with high-polyphenol finishing oil and micro-parsley for visual elevation.

instructions.step: Blend chickpeas with tahini, lemon, garlic and cumin until very smooth.
Warm some whole chickpeas separately.
Spread hummus in a plate. Add whole chickpeas on top.
Drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle paprika and parsley.
Serve with warm pita.

name: Lamb Shawarma
description: Spiced lamb slices in flatbread with tahini, tomatoes and pickled turnip. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with toasted pine nuts for the textural complexity required in professional plating.

instructions.step: Slice lamb thin. Marinate with spices and lemon 1 hour.
Roast at 220°C or grill until charred, 15 min.
Make tahini sauce with tahini, lemon and garlic.
Serve in flatbread with sauce, tomatoes, pickled turnip and parsley.

name: Fattoush Salad
description: Levantine bread salad with crispy pita, sumac, pomegranate molasses and mint. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce Urfa Biber for smoky depth and use ice-chilled radish for maximum crunch.

instructions.step: Toast or fry pita until crispy. Break into pieces.
Tear lettuce. Dice tomatoes and cucumber. Slice radish.
Whisk olive oil, lemon, pomegranate molasses and sumac.
Toss all with dressing and herbs.
Add pita pieces just before serving.

name: Grilled Branzino
description: Whole sea bass grilled with herbs de Provence, capers and lemon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Implement a high-heat sear followed by indirect heat to preserve moisture and achieve crispy, glass-like skin.

instructions.step: Score fish. Season inside and out with herbs, garlic and salt.
Stuff cavity with lemon slices.
Grill 7–8 min per side until cooked through.
Meanwhile roast tomatoes and olives with olive oil.
Serve fish over tomatoes, drizzle with capers and lemon.

name: Lamb Kofta
description: Ground lamb and herb skewers grilled and served with tahini and tabbouleh. ⭐ Pro Tip: Toast whole spices before grinding to unlock volatile oils and depth of flavor.

instructions.step: Mix lamb with grated onion, spices and chopped herbs.
Shape onto flat skewers into sausage shapes.
Grill over high heat 4 min per side.
Serve with tahini, warm pita, tabbouleh and sumac.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce with feta and bread. ⭐ Pro Tip: Bloom spices in oil for 60 seconds to maximize aromatic impact.

instructions.step: Sauté onion and pepper until soft.
Add garlic and spices. Add tomatoes. Simmer 10 min.
Make wells in the sauce. Crack in eggs.
Cover and cook 5–6 min until whites are set.
Crumble feta over, scatter parsley. Serve with pita.

name: Tabbouleh
description: Lebanon's national salad — bulgur wheat with vast amounts of fresh parsley. ⭐ Pro Tip: Seed the tomatoes to prevent a watery base and maintain a crisp texture over time.

instructions.step: Soak bulgur in cold water 20 min. Drain and squeeze dry.
Finely chop parsley and mint — this is mostly herb, not grain.
Dice tomatoes and scallion.
Toss everything with lemon juice, olive oil and allspice.
Rest 20 min before serving.

name: Baba Ganoush
description: Smoky roasted aubergine dip with tahini, lemon and pomegranate seeds. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add Maldon sea salt and a pinch of Urfa Biber for sophisticated heat and mineral balance.

instructions.step: Char aubergine directly over flame or under grill until completely blackened.
Rest 10 min in a bag.
Peel and drain. Chop flesh.
Mix with tahini, lemon and garlic.
Spread on a plate, drizzle oil, scatter pomegranate and parsley.

name: Mansaf
description: Jordan's national dish — lamb in dried yogurt sauce over rice with almonds. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce whole toasted spices into the jameed infusion and finish with fresh parsley to balance the intense sodium levels.

instructions.step: Dissolve jameed in hot water. Simmer lamb in jameed broth 1.5 hours.
Cook rice with turmeric and lamb broth.
Fry nuts in butter until golden.
Layer flatbread, then rice, then lamb on a large platter.
Pour hot jameed sauce over. Scatter nuts.

name: Falafel Wrap
description: Crispy chickpea falafel in flatbread with tahini, tomatoes and pickled cabbage. ⭐ Pro Tip: Elevate the garnish with sumac-onions and a fermented chili element.

instructions.step: Blend chickpeas (raw soaked, not cooked) with herbs, garlic and spices. Add flour.
Shape into balls. Fry at 180°C for 3 min until crispy.
Make tahini sauce: whisk tahini with juice of 1 lemon and enough cold water to make a pourable sauce. Season with salt.
Serve falafel in flatbread with tahini, tomatoes and pickled cabbage.

name: Kibbeh
description: Fried bulgur and lamb croquettes with pine nuts and onion. ⭐ Pro Tip: Toast the pine nuts in lamb fat before adding to the filling for deeper nuttiness.

instructions.step: Mix fine bulgur with half the lamb, onion and spices. Knead to a paste.
Cook remaining lamb with onion, pine nuts and spices for filling.
Shape shells around filling.
Fry at 175°C for 4–5 min until dark golden.
Serve with yogurt sauce.

name: Muhallabia
description: Silky Lebanese milk pudding with rose water and crushed pistachios. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a splash of heavy cream for a richer mouthfeel and strain through a chinois for a flawless finish.

instructions.step: Whisk cornstarch and sugar into cold milk.
Heat stirring constantly until thick, 10 min.
Add rose water, orange blossom water and mastic.
Pour into bowls. Chill 4 hours.
Top with crushed pistachios before serving.

name: Knafeh
description: Palestinian cheese pastry soaked in rose water syrup with pistachios. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap artificial dye for saffron-infused butter and incorporate a blend of Nabulsi cheese for superior elasticity.

instructions.step: Make syrup: boil sugar and water. Add rose water. Cool.
Shred and coat kataifi with melted butter.
Press half into a greased pan. Spread drained cheese.
Top with remaining kataifi.
Bake at 200°C for 25 min until golden.
Pour cold syrup over hot knafeh. Top with pistachios.

name: Chicken Musakhan
description: Sumac-spiced chicken with caramelised onions on flatbread with pine nuts. ⭐ Pro Tip: Deglaze the onion pan with a splash of chicken stock to create a rich emulsion.

instructions.step: Roast chicken thighs at 200°C for 30 min.
Caramelise sliced onions in olive oil with sumac, cinnamon and allspice, 20 min.
Shred chicken. Mix with onions.
Spread flatbread in a pan. Top with chicken mixture.
Warm in oven. Scatter pine nuts and more sumac.

name: Maqluba
description: Upside-down rice layered with lamb, cauliflower and aubergine. ⭐ Pro Tip: Acidify the profile with sumac or lemon zest to balance the heavy lipids.

instructions.step: Fry each vegetable separately until golden.
Brown lamb with spices.
Layer in a deep pot: lamb, fried vegetables, soaked rice.
Add broth to cover. Cook on medium 45 min.
Invert onto a large platter like a cake.
Scatter pine nuts. Serve with yogurt.

name: Labneh
description: Strained yogurt cheese rolled in herbs, drizzled with olive oil. ⭐ Pro Tip: Serve with house-made, charred sourdough pita to meet five-star textural expectations.

instructions.step: Mix yogurt with 1 tsp salt. Tip into a muslin cloth or clean tea towel set over a bowl. Gather the edges, tie, and refrigerate 24 hours until thick and creamy.
Roll into balls or spread on a plate.
Drizzle with olive oil and scatter za'atar and chilli.
Serve with olives, cucumber and warm pita.

name: Fatteh
description: Layers of toasted pita, chickpeas, yogurt-tahini and crispy aubergine. ⭐ Pro Tip: Sizzle the pine nuts in brown butter immediately before serving to provide a dramatic table-side aromatic experience.

instructions.step: Toast pita until crispy. Break into pieces.
Roast diced aubergine in oil at 200°C for 20 min.
Mix yogurt with tahini, garlic and lemon.
Layer: pita, chickpeas, aubergine, yogurt sauce.
Top with pine nuts, pomegranate and parsley.

name: Za'atar Flatbread Manaqeesh
description: Lebanese flatbread spread with za'atar and olive oil — breakfast all over the Levant. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with fresh mint and pomegranate arils for a vibrant, Michelin-standard presentation.

instructions.step: Make the dough: mix 300g flour, 7g instant yeast, 1 tsp salt, 180ml warm water, 2 tbsp olive oil. Knead 5 min, rest 45 min. Or use store-bought flatbread dough.
Divide dough into 4 rounds. Roll thin.
Mix za'atar and olive oil. Spread generously on dough.
Bake at 220°C for 8–10 min until edges are golden.
Serve warm with sliced tomato, cucumber and olives.

name: Grilled Halloumi Salad
description: Golden grilled halloumi over rocket, watermelon and mint. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add toasted pine nuts or pistachios for a necessary fatty crunch.

instructions.step: Slice halloumi 1cm thick. Grill in a dry pan 2 min per side until golden.
Dice watermelon.
Whisk olive oil, lemon and honey.
Arrange rocket, watermelon and halloumi.
Scatter pomegranate and mint. Drizzle dressing.

name: Roasted Red Pepper Muhammara
description: Syrian walnut and roasted pepper dip with pomegranate molasses and cumin. ⭐ Pro Tip: Toast walnuts and cumin seeds prior to processing to unlock essential oils and aromatic complexity.

instructions.step: Blend peppers, walnuts, garlic, spices and pomegranate molasses.
Add olive oil and lemon. Blend until smooth.
Add breadcrumbs to thicken.
Serve drizzled with olive oil and walnut pieces.

name: Sfiha (Meat Flatbread)
description: Open-faced Lebanese flatbread topped with spiced minced lamb. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate finely chopped fresh mint and a pinch of sumac for depth.

instructions.step: Make the dough: mix 300g flour, 7g instant yeast, 1 tsp salt, 180ml warm water, 2 tbsp olive oil. Knead 5 min. Rest 45 min. Divide into 12 small balls. Or use store-bought flatbread dough.
Mix lamb with diced tomatoes, onion and spices.
Divide dough into small rounds. Spread lamb mixture on each.
Scatter pine nuts.
Bake at 220°C for 12 min until edges are golden and meat is cooked.
Squeeze lemon over. Serve with yogurt.

name: Poached Eggs with Labneh and Chilli
description: Slow-poached eggs on labneh with chilli-infused butter and za'atar. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce lemon zest and minced garlic to the labneh for structural acidity.

instructions.step: Spread labneh on plates.
Poach eggs gently in simmering water 3 min.
Melt butter with chilli flakes until sizzling.
Place eggs on labneh. Pour chilli butter over.
Scatter za'atar and parsley. Serve with warm pita.

name: Baklawa
description: Classic Middle Eastern filo pastry layered with pistachios in rose syrup. Store-bought filo pastry is standard — keep sheets covered with a damp cloth as you work. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate ground cardamom into the nut mixture for sophisticated aromatic depth.

instructions.step: Chop nuts together.
Layer 8 filo sheets (buttered between each) in a tray.
Spread nut filling. Top with 8 more buttered sheets.
Score diamond shapes on top. Bake at 170°C for 35 min until golden.
Make syrup: boil sugar and water with rose water and lemon. Pour cold over hot baklawa.
Cool completely before serving.

name: Mujaddara
description: Lebanese lentil and rice pilaf topped with crispy caramelised onions. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a squeeze of lemon and sumac-rubbed red onions to provide essential acidity.

instructions.step: Caramelise onions in olive oil over medium heat 30 min until deeply golden.
Cook lentils 15 min. Drain.
Add rice and lentils to a pot with spices and water. Simmer 20 min.
Serve topped with crispy onions and yogurt.

name: Freekeh Soup
description: Chicken and roasted green wheat soup with warming spices. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce a lemon zest finish and a splash of high-end olive oil to emulsify the broth.

instructions.step: Brown chicken. Saute onion and garlic.
Add freekeh and spices. Toast 2 min.
Add broth. Simmer 25 min.
Shred chicken and return.
Serve with fried pine nuts and parsley.

name: Fatayer
description: Baked Lebanese spinach pies in a lemony sumac pastry. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a finish of Maldon salt and a brush of ghee for a high-gloss, premium presentation.

instructions.step: Make the dough: mix 300g flour, 7g instant yeast, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp sugar, 180ml warm water, 2 tbsp olive oil. Knead until smooth. Rest 1 hr. Divide into 12 balls. Or use store-bought bread dough.
Wilt and squeeze spinach. Mix with onion, sumac, lemon and pine nuts.
Divide dough into balls. Flatten.
Place filling in center. Pinch into triangle shapes.
Bake at 200C for 18 min.

name: Samke Harra
description: Whole baked fish in a spicy walnut and coriander sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a quick broil to crisp the skin while maintaining a succulent, pearlescent interior.

instructions.step: Score fish. Season and bake at 200C for 15 min.
Make sauce: blend walnuts, coriander, garlic and chilli.
Fry sauce in olive oil 3 min.
Pour over fish. Return to oven 10 min.
Squeeze lemon over. Scatter pomegranate.

name: Mjaddara Hamra
description: Red lentil and caramelised onion dish from northern Lebanon. ⭐ Pro Tip: Fold in chopped parsley and toasted pine nuts for textural contrast and aesthetic appeal.

instructions.step: Caramelise onions in olive oil over medium heat 25 min.
Boil lentils until soft, about 12 min.
Combine with onions and cumin.
Mash partially - keep some texture.
Season with lemon and salt. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Tonkotsu Ramen
description: Rich pork-bone broth with chashu pork, soft egg and ramen noodles. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use a pressure cooker to accelerate collagen breakdown if a long simmer is not possible.

instructions.step: Blanch bones, drain and rinse. Simmer in fresh water 4 hours.
Roll pork belly. Braise in soy, mirin, garlic and water 2 hours.
Marinate boiled eggs in soy and mirin 4 hours.
Cook noodles per packet. Divide into bowls.
Ladle hot broth. Top with chashu slices, halved egg, nori, bamboo and spring onion.

name: Chicken Katsu Curry
description: Crispy panko chicken with Japanese golden curry sauce and rice. ⭐ Pro Tip: Implement a 3% salt brine for the chicken to ensure moisture retention during high-heat frying.

instructions.step: Pound chicken thin. Season. Coat in flour, egg then panko.
Deep fry at 170C until golden, 6 min.
Saute onion, carrot, potato. Add water and simmer 15 min.
Add curry roux blocks, stir until dissolved. Simmer 5 min.
Serve katsu sliced over rice with curry sauce.

name: Gyoza
description: Pan-fried pork and cabbage dumplings with dipping sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add Nira and a splash of sake or dashi to the filling for deep umami.

instructions.step: (Use store-bought gyoza wrappers, or make from scratch: mix 200g flour with 100ml boiling water, knead until smooth, rest 30 min, roll thin and cut into circles.) Salt cabbage, squeeze dry. Mix with pork, garlic, ginger, soy and sesame oil.
Place filling in wrappers. Fold and pleat edges to seal.
Pan-fry in oil flat-side down 3 min until golden.
Add 60ml water, cover and steam 4 min.
Mix dipping sauce: soy, rice vinegar, chilli oil. Serve immediately.

name: Chirashi Sushi Bowl
description: Seasoned sushi rice topped with sashimi, avocado and pickled ginger. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce ikura for salinity and shiso leaves for aromatic contrast.

instructions.step: Cook rice. Season with vinegar, sugar and salt. Fan cool.
Slice fish and avocado.
Arrange rice in bowls. Arrange toppings.
Scatter sesame, serve with soy, wasabi and pickled ginger.

name: Miso Soup
description: Warming dashi broth with tofu, wakame and spring onion. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with micro-herbs or yuzu zest to elevate the sensory experience beyond home-home cooking.

instructions.step: Rehydrate wakame in cold water 5 min. Drain.
Bring 1 liter water to simmer. Dissolve dashi powder.
Remove from heat. Whisk in miso paste.
Add cubed tofu and wakame.
Serve topped with sliced spring onion.

name: Takoyaki
description: Crispy octopus balls with bonito flakes and tangy sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a drizzle of truffle-infused soy and high-grade katsuobushi to elevate the dish beyond street-food origins.

instructions.step: Make the batter: whisk 120g plain flour, 1 tsp baking powder, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 egg and 400ml dashi until smooth. Or use store-bought takoyaki batter mix — prepare per packet instructions.
Mix batter with dashi and eggs until smooth.
Heat takoyaki pan. Oil each cup.
Fill cups with batter. Add octopus and scallion.
Rotate balls as edges cook. Full rotation 8 min.
Drizzle sauce and mayo. Top with bonito, ginger and aonori.

name: Teriyaki Salmon
description: Glossy teriyaki-glazed salmon with steamed rice and edamame. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate micro-shaved ginger and a yuzu squeeze to brighten the heavy sauce profile.

instructions.step: Mix soy, mirin, sake and sugar. Simmer 3 min to glaze consistency.
Pan-sear salmon skin-side down 4 min.
Flip, pour glaze over. Cook 3 min basting.
Cook rice. Steam edamame.
Serve salmon on rice with edamame and sesame seeds.

name: Okonomiyaki
description: Savoury Japanese pancake with cabbage, pork and umami toppings. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a dusting of Aonori powder and house-made dashi-infused sauce to elevate the flavor profile beyond standard retail products.

instructions.step: Mix flour, dashi and eggs. Fold in shredded cabbage and ginger.
Pour into hot oiled pan. Lay pork on top.
Cook 5 min. Flip carefully. Cook 5 more min.
Drizzle sauce and mayo in crosses.
Top with bonito flakes and spring onion.

name: Yakitori
description: Grilled chicken skewers glazed with sweet tare sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate binchotan charcoal for authentic smoky depth and finish with a touch of yuzu kosho to elevate the flavor profile.

instructions.step: Simmer soy, mirin, sake and sugar until syrupy tare sauce forms.
Cut chicken and spring onion into 3cm pieces. Thread alternately on skewers.
Grill on high heat, basting with tare, 12 min total.
Sprinkle sesame and serve immediately.

name: Tempura Vegetables
description: Light, crispy battered vegetables with tentsuyu dipping sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate sparkling ice water for superior aeration and use a neutral oil with a higher smoke point like grapeseed.

instructions.step: Make tentsuyu: simmer dashi, soy and mirin together 3 min. Remove from heat and cool.
Mix flour, cornstarch, egg and ice water - do not overmix.
Heat oil to 180°C — takes about 5 min. Mix batter at the last moment: combine flour, cornstarch, egg and ice water — do not overmix. Dip vegetables.
Fry in small batches 2–3 min per batch until pale golden and crisp. Work quickly to keep batter cold between batches.
Drain on paper. Serve with tentsuyu and grated daikon.

name: Japanese Curry Rice
description: Golden curry with chicken, potato and carrot over steamed rice. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace jasmine rice with Koshihikari for authentic texture and finish with a cold butter mount for a glossy, 5-star sheen.

instructions.step: Brown chicken. Remove.
Saute onion, garlic and ginger until soft.
Add potato and carrot. Add 600ml water. Bring to boil.
Simmer 20 min. Add curry roux blocks, stir until smooth.
Add chicken back. Simmer 5 min. Serve over rice.

name: Salmon Onigiri
description: Triangular rice balls filled with flaked salmon and wrapped in nori. ⭐ Pro Tip: Integrate toasted sesame oil or fresh shiso leaves into the filling for aromatic depth.

instructions.step: Cook and season rice with vinegar and salt.
Pan-fry salmon. Flake and toss with soy sauce.
With wet, salted hands form rice into triangles around salmon filling.
Wrap base of each with nori strip.
Sprinkle sesame and serve or wrap for lunchbox.

name: Matcha Cheesecake
description: Silky Japanese baked cheesecake with earthy matcha and graham base. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace the cracker crust with a toasted almond or yuzu-infused shortbread for a sophisticated crunch.

instructions.step: Crush crackers with butter. Press into tin. Bake 10 min.
Beat cream cheese, sugar and vanilla until smooth.
Mix matcha with 2 tbsp hot water. Blend in.
Add eggs one at a time. Fold in cream.
Pour over base. Bake in water bath at 160C for 50 min.
Cool in oven with door ajar. Refrigerate overnight.

name: Edamame with Sea Salt
description: Boiled soya beans in pods seasoned with coarse sea salt. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with a touch of toasted sesame oil and yuzu zest for complex aromatic depth.

instructions.step: Bring salted water to boil. Add edamame, cook 5 min.
Drain. Toss with sea salt and chilli flakes.
Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Beef Sukiyaki
description: Hot-pot beef and vegetables simmered in sweet soy broth. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate beef tallow for the initial sear to enhance umami depth and replace sugar with a touch of pear puree for natural sweetness.

instructions.step: Make warishita: mix soy, mirin, sake and sugar. Bring to boil.
Heat sukiyaki pan. Sear a few beef slices first.
Add warishita, then vegetables, tofu and noodles.
Simmer together, adding more sauce as needed.
Each person beats a raw egg and dips cooked ingredients.

name: Oyakodon
description: Chicken and egg simmered in dashi and soy - the classic donburi. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute Jasmine with premium short-grain rice and introduce Mitsuba for a more sophisticated aromatic profile.

instructions.step: Cook rice.
Slice chicken and onion. Simmer in dashi, soy, mirin and sugar 8 min.
Pour lightly beaten eggs over. Cover and cook 2 min - keep runny.
Slide over rice bowls.
Top with sliced spring onion and shredded nori.

name: Agedashi Tofu
description: Silken tofu in crisp coating served in umami dashi broth. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with high-quality katsuobushi flakes to add essential smoky umami and visual movement.

instructions.step: Drain tofu on paper towels 30 min. Cut into cubes.
Make broth: simmer dashi, soy and mirin.
Coat tofu in cornstarch. Deep fry at 180C until crispy, 4 min.
Place tofu in bowls. Pour hot broth around.
Top with grated daikon, ginger and spring onion.

name: Katsudon
description: Crispy pork cutlet simmered with egg in sweet soy over rice. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap jasmine for Koshihikari rice and use pork tenderloin for a more refined, lean finish.

instructions.step: Pound pork thin. Coat in flour, beaten egg, panko. Fry until golden, 6 min.
Simmer onion in dashi, soy, mirin and sugar 5 min.
Slice katsu. Place in pan with onions.
Pour beaten egg over. Cover and cook 90 sec.
Slide over rice. Serve immediately.

name: Shabu Shabu
description: Japanese hot pot - thin beef slices swished in kombu broth. ⭐ Pro Tip: Upgrade to a blended dashi using katsuobushi and use 'Momiji Oroshi' (chili-spiked daikon) to sharpen the ponzu's acidity.

instructions.step: Soak kombu in cold water 30 min. Heat to just under boiling. Remove kombu.
Arrange raw ingredients at table.
Each person swishes thin beef slices in broth 10-15 sec.
Dip in ponzu or sesame sauce with daikon and spring onion.
Add vegetables and noodles to broth as you go.

name: Japanese Potato Salad
description: Creamy mashed potato salad with cucumber, carrot and Japanese mayo. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce Japanese hot mustard (karashi) and high-quality ham to create complex layers of flavor.

instructions.step: Boil potatoes until soft. Drain and mash roughly.
Hard boil eggs. Cool, peel and chop.
Salt cucumber, squeeze dry. Salt onion, rinse and squeeze.
Combine all with mayo, vinegar, salt and pepper.
Refrigerate at least 30 min.

name: Shoyu Ramen
description: Clear soy broth ramen with chashu, menma and a soft egg. ⭐ Pro Tip: Develop a concentrated tare and an infused aroma oil to elevate the flavor profile.

instructions.step: Combine broth, soy and mirin. Simmer 10 min. Add sesame oil.
Cook noodles per packet. Divide into bowls.
Ladle hot broth.
Top with sliced chashu, halved egg, bamboo, nori and spring onion.

name: Natto Rice
description: Fermented soya beans stirred with soy, mustard and spring onion over rice. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap jasmine for seasoned short-grain rice and introduce kizami wasabi or pickled daikon for acidic contrast.

instructions.step: Cook rice.
Stir natto vigorously with soy and mustard until stringy and fragrant.
Spoon over rice. Top with spring onion and egg yolk.
Serve with torn nori on the side.

name: Karaage Chicken
description: Japanese fried chicken - juicy, crispy and deeply flavored. ⭐ Pro Tip: Serve with a yuzu-kosho infused mayonnaise to add a sophisticated, acidic counterpoint to the rich fat.

instructions.step: Cut chicken into chunks. Marinate in soy, sake, ginger and garlic 30 min.
Mix cornstarch and flour. Coat chicken.
Fry at 175C in batches for 5 min until golden.
Rest 3 min. Fry again 1 min for extra crunch.
Serve with lemon wedges and Japanese mayo.

name: Zaru Soba
description: Cold buckwheat noodles with dipping broth and wasabi. ⭐ Pro Tip: Shock the noodles in an ice-water bath to achieve the signature snap and elite texture.

instructions.step: Make tsuyu: simmer dashi, soy and mirin. Cool to room temp.
Cook soba per packet. Drain and rinse under cold water until cold.
Arrange noodles on bamboo tray or plate.
Garnish with nori strips, sesame and spring onion.
Serve tsuyu alongside with wasabi for dipping.

name: Tamagoyaki
description: Rolled sweet omelette - a Japanese breakfast staple. ⭐ Pro Tip: Lightly reduce sugar and add a pinch of sea salt to elevate the egg's natural profile.

instructions.step: Whisk eggs with dashi, soy, mirin and sugar.
Heat rectangular pan. Lightly oil.
Pour thin layer of egg. When just set, roll from one end.
Push roll to one side. Oil pan. Pour next layer, lifting roll to let egg flow under.
Repeat 3 times, rolling each time. Slice into rounds.

name: Tonkatsu
description: Deep-fried panko pork cutlet with shredded cabbage and tonkatsu sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Season the pork directly before dredging and shock the shredded cabbage in ice water for maximum crunch.

instructions.step: Pound pork to 1cm. Score fat edge to prevent curling.
Coat in flour, beaten egg then panko.
Fry at 170C until golden, 6 min.
Rest on wire rack 2 min.
Slice and serve on shredded cabbage with tonkatsu sauce and mustard.

name: Dorayaki
description: Fluffy pancake sandwiches filled with sweet red bean paste. ⭐ Pro Tip: Introduce sea salt and swap water for milk to enhance richness and moisture.

instructions.step: Mix eggs, sugar, honey, mirin and water. Sift in flour and baking powder.
Rest batter 15 min.
Cook small pancakes in lightly oiled pan, 2 min each side.
Cool. Sandwich two pancakes around a spoonful of red bean paste.

name: Mochi Ice Cream
description: Soft glutinous rice cake wrapped around balls of ice cream. ⭐ Pro Tip: Whisk the matcha into the dry flour for uniform color and replace cornstarch with potato starch to avoid a chalky finish.

instructions.step: Scoop ice cream into balls. Freeze solid 2 hours.
Mix rice flour, sugar and water. Microwave 2 min, stir, microwave 1 more min.
Turn out onto cornstarch-dusted surface. Divide into 8 portions.
Flatten each. Wrap around an ice cream ball. Pinch to seal.
Serve immediately or freeze up to 1 week.

name: Chawanmushi
description: Silky steamed egg custard with chicken, prawn and shiitake. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate ginkgo nuts for authentic texture and par-blanch the chicken to ensure a perfectly clean finish without clouding the custard.

instructions.step: Mix eggs with dashi, soy and mirin. Strain through fine sieve.
Divide chicken, prawns and mushrooms into 4 cups.
Pour custard carefully over. Cover with foil.
Steam on very low heat 20 min - do not boil or it will bubble.
Garnish with spring onion. Serve warm.

name: Japanese Cotton Cheesecake
description: Ultra-light, cotton-soft baked cheesecake - jiggly and delicate. ⭐ Pro Tip: Infuse the milk with fresh lemon zest to brighten the heavy lipid profile of the cream cheese.

instructions.step: Melt cream cheese, butter and milk over low heat until smooth. Cool.
Whisk in egg yolks, flour, cornstarch, lemon and vanilla.
Beat whites with sugar to stiff peaks. Fold into batter gently in thirds.
Pour into lined tin. Bake in water bath at 160C for 55 min.
Cool in oven 10 min with door ajar before removing.

name: Bibimbap
description: Rice topped with seasoned vegetables, gochujang and a fried egg. ⭐ Pro Tip: Finish with premium, cold-pressed toasted sesame oil added only at the moment of service for maximum aromatics.

instructions.step: Cook rice.
Blanch spinach, squeeze and season with sesame oil and garlic.
Julienne and saute carrot and courgette separately.
Saute mushrooms with soy.
Fry eggs sunny side up.
Arrange rice in bowls, top with vegetables and egg. Serve with gochujang and sesame seeds.

name: Bulgogi
description: Thin-sliced marinated beef grilled at the table. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a splash of toasted sesame oil right before serving to preserve volatile aromatics.

instructions.step: Blend pear, garlic and ginger. Mix with soy, sugar and sesame oil.
Slice beef paper thin. Marinate 30 min.
Grill on high heat 2-3 min per side.
Serve with lettuce wraps, rice and kimchi.
Scatter sesame seeds and spring onion.

name: Kimchi Jjigae
description: Fiery kimchi stew with tofu and pork - Korean soul food. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace water with an anchovy-dashima stock and use kimchi aged at least six months.

instructions.step: Brown pork belly in pot.
Add kimchi and gochugaru. Stir fry 3 min.
Add 600ml water, gochujang, soy and garlic.
Simmer 15 min.
Add tofu, simmer 5 more min.
Finish with sesame oil and spring onion. Serve with rice.

name: Tteokbokki
description: Chewy rice cakes in a sweet and spicy gochujang sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace water with a rich dried anchovy and kombu stock to elevate the sauce complexity.

instructions.step: Soak tteok in cold water 10 min if hard.
Mix gochujang, gochugaru, sugar and soy with 500ml water in pot. Bring to boil.
Add tteok and fish cakes. Simmer 10 min stirring until sauce thickens.
Garnish with spring onion, sesame and halved boiled eggs.

name: Japchae
description: Glass noodles stir-fried with vegetables and sesame. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace refined sugar with mirin and honey to glaze the noodles with a more refined, glossy finish.

instructions.step: Soak noodles in hot water 10 min. Drain.
Saute each vegetable separately with garlic.
Pan-fry thin egg strips.
Toss noodles with soy, sesame oil and sugar over high heat.
Combine with vegetables. Top with sesame seeds.

name: Doenjang Jjigae
description: Fermented soybean paste stew with tofu, courgette and mushrooms. ⭐ Pro Tip: Bloom gochugaru and garlic in sesame oil before adding stock to deepen the aromatic profile.

instructions.step: Bring anchovy stock to simmer.
Add doenjang, stir to dissolve.
Add potato and onion. Cook 8 min.
Add courgette, mushrooms, tofu and garlic.
Simmer 8 min. Finish with gochugaru and spring onion.

name: Samgyeopsal
description: Grilled pork belly wraps with garlic, ssamjang and perilla leaves. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use high-quality maldon salt and cold-pressed sesame oil to elevate the dipping sauce.

instructions.step: Grill pork belly slices on hot griddle, turning often until crispy, 12 min.
Grill garlic slices alongside until golden.
Wrap pork, garlic and kimchi in lettuce with perilla leaves and ssamjang.
Dip in sesame oil and salt.

name: Sundubu Jjigae
description: Extra-soft tofu stew with seafood in spicy broth - egg cracked in at the end. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with micro-scallions and chili threads for refined visual appeal.

instructions.step: Saute garlic and onion in sesame oil.
Add gochugaru, toast 1 min.
Add stock. Bring to boil.
Add seafood and tofu in large pieces. Simmer 8 min.
Crack an egg into each individual pot. Cover and cook 1 min.
Season with soy. Top with spring onion.

name: Gimbap
description: Korean seaweed rice rolls with egg, spinach, carrot and fish cake. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute a portion of white rice with pressed barley to improve the glycemic index.

instructions.step: Cook and cool rice. Season with sesame oil and salt.
Make omelette strips. Blanch and season spinach. Saute carrot. Pan-fry fish cake strips.
Place nori on bamboo mat. Spread rice leaving 3cm gap at top.
Arrange fillings in a row. Roll tightly. Seal edge with water.
Slice into rounds. Brush with sesame oil.

name: Dakgalbi
description: Stir-fried spicy chicken with tteok, cabbage and sweet potato. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add grated ginger and perilla leaves for a sophisticated flavor profile; finish with a torch-sear to provide the authentic smoky char exp...

instructions.step: Mix gochujang, gochugaru, soy, sugar, garlic and sesame oil into a marinade.
Toss chicken with half the marinade. Let sit 15 min.
Stir-fry chicken on high heat with sweet potato and tteok.
Add cabbage and onion and remaining marinade. Cook 10 min.
Serve with rice.

name: Haemul Pajeon
description: Crispy seafood and spring onion pancake with soy dipping sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute ice water with chilled sparkling water for better aeration and use a weighted press for even browning.

instructions.step: Mix flours, eggs and ice water to thin batter.
Add sliced spring onions and seafood.
Heat oil in pan. Pour batter. Press flat.
Cook 4 min until base is golden. Flip. Cook 3 more min.
Mix dipping sauce: soy, vinegar, sesame oil and chilli. Serve hot.

name: Kongnamul Guk
description: Clear soybean sprout soup - a comforting Korean morning staple. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace water with a concentrated anchovy-dashima broth and add a splash of fish sauce for luxury-grade salinity.

instructions.step: Bring 800ml water to boil. Add rinsed sprouts. Do not open lid for 5 min.
Add garlic, soy and salt.
Simmer 8 min.
Finish with gochugaru, sesame oil and spring onion.

name: Bossam
description: Slow-boiled pork belly served with cabbage wraps and fermented condiments. ⭐ Pro Tip: Brine the napa cabbage in salt for two hours to ensure flexibility and seasoning.

instructions.step: Boil pork belly in water with doenjang, ginger, garlic and spring onion 70 min.
Rest 10 min. Slice thin.
Serve with raw cabbage leaves, kimchi, ssamjang, oysters and sliced garlic.
Wrap each slice in a cabbage leaf with condiments.

name: Gamjatang
description: Pork neck bone soup with potato, perilla and gochugaru. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate aromatics like ginger and black pepper during the initial simmer to eliminate gaminess and refine the broth profile.

instructions.step: Blanch bones 5 min. Drain, rinse.
Simmer in fresh water with garlic 60 min.
Add potatoes and pastes. Simmer 20 min until potatoes are soft.
Finish with perilla, spring onion, sesame and sesame oil.

name: Hotteok
description: Pan-fried sweet pancakes filled with brown sugar, cinnamon and nuts. ⭐ Pro Tip: Use clarified butter for frying and finish with a sprinkle of smoked sea salt to elevate the flavor profile.

instructions.step: Mix flour, yeast, warm water and sugar. Knead until smooth. Rest 1 hour.
Mix brown sugar, cinnamon and crushed walnuts.
Divide dough into 8 balls. Flatten. Add a spoonful of filling. Seal.
Pan-fry in oil over medium heat. Press flat with spatula.
Cook 2 min each side until golden.

name: Kimchi Fried Rice
description: Fried rice with kimchi, gochujang and sesame - topped with fried egg. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate fermented shrimp paste or kimchi brine for a deeper umami punch and acidity.

instructions.step: (Use day-old cooked rice for best results — freshly cooked rice is too wet. Cook 300g jasmine rice, spread on a tray and refrigerate uncovered overnight.) Stir-fry garlic in sesame oil. Add kimchi and gochujang. Cook 3 min.
Add rice, stir-fry on high heat 4 min.
Season with soy. Toss well.
Fry eggs separately.
Serve rice topped with fried egg, sesame seeds, spring onion and shredded nori.

name: Korean Cucumber Salad
description: Thinly sliced cucumber in sweet vinegar dressing with sesame. ⭐ Pro Tip: Reduce sugar by half and add a splash of fish sauce for umami.

instructions.step: Slice cucumber paper thin. Salt and rest 10 min. Squeeze out water.
Mix vinegar, sugar, sesame oil and gochugaru.
Toss cucumber in dressing.
Garnish with sesame seeds. Chill 30 min.

name: Gyeranjjim
description: Silky steamed Korean egg custard in a stone bowl. ⭐ Pro Tip: Substitute salt with fine-minced Saeujeot for a punch of fermented umami and more complex salinity.

instructions.step: Beat eggs with cooled anchovy stock, soy and salt until smooth.
Pour into stone bowl or ramekins.
Steam on medium heat 12 min until just set.
Drizzle sesame oil. Garnish with spring onion and sesame seeds.

name: Dakjuk
description: Korean chicken congee - smooth rice porridge with shredded chicken. ⭐ Pro Tip: Garnish with high-quality toasted seaweed and a drizzle of premium cold-pressed sesame oil to elevate the finish.

instructions.step: Simmer chicken with garlic and ginger in 1.5L water 25 min.
Remove chicken. Shred. Reserve broth.
Add washed rice to broth. Simmer stirring 20 min until porridge.
Return shredded chicken.
Serve with sesame oil, soy, spring onion and sesame seeds.

name: Jajangmyeon
description: Noodles in black bean sauce with pork and vegetables. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a cornstarch slurry for a professional sheen and introduce ginger and scallions to the aromatic base.

instructions.step: Stir-fry chunjang in oil 3 min to remove bitterness.
Add pork, brown. Add vegetables. Cook 5 min.
Add 300ml water, oyster sauce and sugar. Simmer 10 min.
Cook noodles per packet. Divide.
Pour sauce over noodles. Garnish with julienned cucumber. Drizzle sesame oil.

name: Banchan Spread
description: A classic spread of Korean small side dishes - spinach, cucumber and bean sprouts. ⭐ Pro Tip: Correct the nutritional data to reflect the high oil density.

instructions.step: Blanch spinach. Squeeze dry. Season with garlic, sesame oil and soy.
Salt cucumber. Squeeze. Toss with gochugaru, sesame and vinegar.
Blanch sprouts 2 min. Toss with sesame oil, soy and garlic.
Arrange in small dishes. Refrigerate. Serve alongside rice and main dishes.

name: Mandu
description: Korean dumplings pan-fried or steamed with pork and tofu. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate cold pork aspic for a 'soup dumpling' effect and use a high-heat flash-fry after steaming to restore the maillard crust.

instructions.step: (Use store-bought dumpling wrappers, or make from scratch: mix 200g flour with 100ml boiling water, knead 5 min, rest 30 min, roll thin and cut into circles.) Press and drain tofu. Salt cabbage, squeeze dry.
Mix pork, tofu, cabbage, spring onion, garlic, ginger, soy, sesame and gochugaru.
Fill wrappers with a teaspoon of filling. Fold and pleat to seal.
Pan-fry flat-side down in oil 3 min. Add 50ml water. Cover, steam 3 min.
Serve with soy dipping sauce.

name: Yukhoe
description: Korean beef tartare with pear, sesame oil and egg yolk. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate a finishing touch of flaky sea salt right before serving to make the flavors pop.

instructions.step: Slice beef into thin strips. Chill well.
Julienne pear.
Mix beef with garlic, soy, sesame oil, sugar, gochugaru and sesame seeds.
Arrange on plates with pear.
Top each portion with an egg yolk and pine nuts.

name: Oi Sobagi
description: Spicy stuffed cucumber kimchi - crunchy, tangy and fresh. ⭐ Pro Tip: Swap refined sugar for grated Asian pear to elevate sweetness and introduce garlic chives (buchu) for superior aromatic intensity.

instructions.step: Quarter cucumbers lengthwise keeping base intact. Salt 20 min.
Mix garlic, ginger, gochugaru, fish sauce and sugar.
Add julienned spring onion and carrot to paste.
Rinse cucumbers. Stuff filling into each cucumber.
Eat immediately or ferment in fridge 1 day.

name: Sigeumchi Namul
description: Blanched spinach seasoned with sesame oil, garlic and soy. ⭐ Pro Tip: Reduce blanching time and aggressively squeeze out moisture to prevent seasoning dilution.

instructions.step: Blanch spinach 1 min. Rinse under cold water. Squeeze thoroughly.
Season with garlic, sesame oil, soy and salt.
Toss well. Top with sesame seeds.

name: Doenjang Glazed Aubergine
description: Pan-roasted aubergine with fermented soybean paste glaze. ⭐ Pro Tip: Add a splash of mirin to the glaze for shine and balance, and finish with fresh perilla or micro-greens for a more sophisticated presenta...

instructions.step: Halve aubergine. Score flesh in crosshatch.
Pan-fry cut-side down in oil until golden, 6 min.
Mix doenjang, honey, garlic, sesame oil and gochugaru.
Brush over aubergine. Cook 8 more min in oven at 200C.
Top with sesame seeds and spring onion.

name: Pajeon
description: Crispy spring onion pancake with soy sesame dipping sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Incorporate calamari or bay shrimp to elevate the dish to Michelin-star complexity and increase protein density.

instructions.step: Mix flours, eggs and ice water into thin batter. Do not overmix.
Lay spring onions in hot oiled pan. Pour batter over.
Cook 4 min until base is golden. Flip. Cook 3 min.
Mix dipping sauce: soy, sesame oil, vinegar and gochugaru.
Cut into squares. Serve immediately with sauce.

name: Galbi
description: Grilled Korean short ribs marinated in soy, pear and sesame. ⭐ Pro Tip: Opt for LA-style flanken cuts to achieve better surface caramelization.

instructions.step: Score ribs between bones. Soak in cold water 30 min.
Blend pear, garlic, ginger. Mix with soy, sugar, sesame oil.
Marinate ribs 4 hours or overnight.
Grill on high heat 5-6 min per side.
Scatter sesame and spring onion. Serve with lettuce wraps and rice.

name: Patbingsu
description: Shaved ice dessert with sweet red bean, mochi, condensed milk and fruit. ⭐ Pro Tip: Replace sesame seeds with toasted soybean powder (Injeolmi) and add a pinch of sea salt to balance the sugar.

instructions.step: Shave or blend ice finely.
Divide into bowls. Top with red bean paste.
Add mochi, sliced fruit and sesame seeds.
Drizzle condensed milk. Serve immediately.

name: Soft Tofu Bowl
description: Silken tofu in gochugaru broth topped with sesame and spring onion. ⭐ Pro Tip: Serve in a preheated stone dolsot to maintain the theatrical simmer and properly cook the egg.

instructions.step: Saute garlic in sesame oil. Add gochugaru, stir 1 min.
Add broth and soy. Bring to boil.
Add tofu in large scoops. Simmer 8 min.
Crack an egg into each bowl.
Top with sesame seeds and spring onion. Serve with rice.

name: Kung Pao Chicken
description: Stir-fried chicken with Sichuan peppercorns, dried chillies and peanuts. ⭐ Pro Tip: Reduce sugar by 25% and incorporate toasted cashews as a premium alternative to peanuts for a more refined mouthfeel.

instructions.step: Marinate chicken in soy, wine and cornstarch 15 min.
Stir-fry peppercorns and chillies in hot wok until fragrant.
Add chicken. Stir-fry on high heat 4 min.
Add garlic, ginger, peanuts and sauce (soy, vinegar, sugar).
Toss. Finish with spring onion and sesame oil.

name: Dim Sum Basket
description: A steamer basket of har gow, siu mai and char siu bao. ⭐ Pro Tip: Shorten steaming time to 7 minutes to preserve the snap of the prawns.

instructions.step: (Use store-bought har gow and siu mai wrappers. Scratch wrappers: mix 150g wheat starch, 50g tapioca starch, 200ml boiling water; knead, roll thin and cut.) Har gow filling: minced prawns, bamboo, sesame oil. Fill and pleat.
Siu mai filling: pork, prawn, ginger, oyster sauce. Fill and shape open-topped.
Arrange in lined bamboo steamers.
Steam 12-14 min.
Serve with chilli oil and soy sauce for dipping.

name: Mapo Tofu
description: Silken tofu in a fiery Sichuan doubanjiang and minced pork sauce. ⭐ Pro Tip: Blanch tofu in salted water for 60 seconds to improve elasticity and heat retention.

instructions.step: Toast Sichuan peppercorns. Grind coarsely.
Brown pork. Add doubanjiang, garlic and ginger. Cook 3 min.
Add broth and soy. Bring to simmer.
Add tofu in cubes gently. Simmer 5 min.
Thicken with cornstarch slurry. Finish with spring onion, peppercorns and chilli oil.

name: Char Siu Pork
description: Cantonese BBQ pork with sticky red lacquer glaze. ⭐ Pro Tip: Switch honey for maltose to achieve a professional glass-like finish and incorporate red bean curd for authentic umami.

instructions.step: Mix all marinade ingredients. Coat pork. Marinate 4 hours or overnight.
Roast at 220C on a rack for 25 min.
Brush with remaining marinade every 8 min.
Rest 5 min. Slice across the grain.
Serve with steamed rice and bok choy.
